programme.title: NCIS

Held hostage as a bargaining chip, he tries to identify the real culprit before the warden takes drastic action to resolve the crisis

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles

Chris O'Donnell and LL Cool J star

programme.desc: Peggy attends a district nurses' conference in Scarborough, where she surprises everyone - including herself - with her instinctive knack for public speaking. programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Nate's day begins in high spirits with a driving assessment supervised by Tony, but events escalate when he fails to keep up with a stolen car, which is later involved in a hit-and-run. The investigations lead to the driver, but also reveal the victim's family has been under intolerable strain, and her brother has been making ends meet by dabbling in motor theft and drug-running

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Malick blames himself when Paul's brother dies, but the ordeal is not yet over, and the volatile medic is left anxiously waiting to see whether his ex-lover can recover from kidney failure. Meanwhile, an encounter with a happily married couple prompts Chrissie to contemplate her relationship with Dan again, and Jac takes out her frustration on newcomer Kip Maxwell after losing baby Freya. Guest starring Maureen Lipman

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Part two of two. Nathan meets a mysterious stranger named Stitch when a croupier is attacked, and soon comes to respect his medical abilities. However, Selena and Guppy are less impressed by the newcomer when he pressures them into following his diagnosis of the patient. Ben Price, Peter O'Brien and Elizabeth Carling star

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of two. A teenage runaway is found dead from an overdose near a known drug users' haunt and fingerprints on the syringe suggest she was murdered. Investigations lead Mickey and Jo to a gang trafficking young girls which is part of a national network, and Jo resolves there will be no more victims. Warren Brown guest stars. Concludes tomorrow

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Grant agrees to go on holiday with Tiffany but doesn't know it's with Bianca and Ricky. Lorraine fusses over Joe due to Sarah's imminent departure. Ted decides to go to Dubai alone

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Annie's angry when George makes a deal with an old friend. Grant tells Phil he must finish with Lorna. Tony and Simon move into Ted's house just as Irene turns up with a surprise

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: Sarah and George begin a secret affair and meet at the cottage. However, Anna hopes that if she can push her father towards Sarah that he will be more open to learning about her relationship with Gino, but she is frustrated over the arrival of her aunt Regina. Meanwhile, James struggles with his emotions. Romantic drama, starring Marta Dusseldorp

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: The vets are puzzled when Farmer Biggins asks them to treat his perfectly healthy cows, while Tristan discovers the object of his affections sings in the choral society. Rural drama, starring Christopher Timothy, Robert Hardy and Peter Davison

programme.title: Dangerfield
programme.desc: Part one of two. Paul's first family holiday in six years turns out to be more business than pleasure when a suspected suicide arouses his suspicions. Drama, starring Nigel Le Vaillant

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Sharon and Tracey's heating breaks down, leaving them with no one to turn to - except their man-hungry neighbour Dorien. Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph

programme.title: Hi-de-Hi!
programme.desc: Peggy the chalet maid tells Jeffrey she has a burning ambition to become a Yellowcoat, so he arranges an interview for her. Comedy, starring Su Pollard, Simon Cadell, Ruth Madoc, Paul Shane and Jeffrey Holland

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth continues her search for a small country retreat - but her idea of small is not quite the same as everybody else's. Comedy, starring Patricia Routledge and Clive Swift

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Howard has an unusual gift for Marina's garden but must deliver it without Pearl finding out, and turns to his friends for help. Long-running Pennines comedy, starring Peter Sallis, Robert Fyfe and Jean Fergusson

programme.title: Inspector Alleyn Mysteries
programme.desc: The investigation into the murder of a model at a summer school for artists leads Inspector Alleyn to encounter a painter he met during his holiday. Pilot episode of the crime drama featuring Ngaio Marsh's aristocratic detective, starring Patrick Malahide

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Jack examines a case previously undertaken by a deceased colleague, who was convinced that the alleged suicide of a journalist was a cover-up by the American government. Enlisting the help of a UFO-spotter, the team discovers the reporter witnessed a mysterious air crash near a US Air Force base, and the retired officers try to track down the original personnel to get some answers. Guest starring Mark Williams, Glynis Barber, Michael Brandon and Raquel Cassidy, with Amanda Redman

programme.title: Taggart
programme.desc: The murder of a boxer leads the team into a murky world of cage fighting, illegal betting and lap dancing. As the investigation unfolds, it appears the victim was living a lie, and Ross has to face a few home truths of his own when his gambling addiction is exposed. Detective drama, guest starring Philip Whitchurch (My Hero), David McKay (Rab C Nesbitt) and Robert Cavanah (The Royal)

programme.title: The Heart Guy
programme.desc: Matt and Charlie's marriage is put to the test when they disagree over Ivy's future, and Hugh's plan to leave Whyhope is scuppered when he is accused of malpractice

programme.title: The Heart Guy
programme.desc: Ajax takes desperate measures to stop Hugh selling the farm, Penny discovers her perfect match thanks to Meryl's dating service, and Matt gets drunk with April

programme.title: Life on Mars
programme.desc: Gene investigates the shooting of a Ugandan Asian man and jumps to the conclusion that the attack is drug-related when he finds a suspicious bag of powder on the victim. When the substance turns out to be heroin, the policeman becomes determined to keep it off the streets of Manchester - and decides to do whatever it takes to get his way. Fantasy drama, starring Philip Glenister and John Simm

Meanwhile, Sam spends time with the paramedics, and a fireman with a debilitating illness contemplates suicide. Peter O'Brien, Luke Bailey and Susan Cookson star

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part two of two. Jo bonds with a teenager being groomed by the traffickers and Mickey leads a sting to catch the ringleader of the operation. But when the alarm is raised too early, they have insufficient evidence to hold their man, and news then reaches the police that the gang is moving a large group of girls to Glasgow. The race is on to track them down, but Jo's anxiety to save the children clouds her professional judgement

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Tiffany receives flowers from an unwelcome admirer. Simon is made to feel excluded by Irene's family reunion meal. Grant tries to convince Phil a trip to Paris is just what he needs

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Tiffany is in a panic about the marriage blessing. Mark is suffering from the side-effects of his medication and cannot go with Ruth to the fostering class

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: George is asked to host a Japanese trade delegation on the eve of Remembrance Day. Knowing how the community will react, he asks Jack to come to the reception in the hope that the approval of a former Japanese prisoner of war might help deal with any ill feeling. At the event Regina is openly hostile toward Sarah, which she attributes to anti-Semitism. Anna's prolonged absence from Ash Park has Gino fearing that their relationship is over, but he is unaware that she is with Caroline awaiting the results of a pregnancy test. Meanwhile, James and Olivia continue to grow closer. Romantic drama, starring Marta Dusseldorp

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: The vets have their work cut out after a herd of dairy farm cows becomes seriously ill, and Tristan's metal detector proves surprisingly useful. Rural drama, starring Christopher Timothy, Robert Hardy and Peter Davison

programme.title: Dangerfield
programme.desc: Part two of two. Paul learns more about the suspicious death of Susan Wood, and someone breaks into the health centre and assaults a doctor

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: As the bills pile up, Tracey has to consider selling Darryl's car, while Sharon goes into the perfume business to raise some cash. Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke and Linda Robson

programme.title: Hi-de-Hi!
programme.desc: When Spike's sing-along routines are considered to have failed, Jeffrey treats the campers to a classical music recital using his collection of gramophone records. However, it's left to Ted to give the campers what they really want without hurting the entertainment manager's feelings. Classic comedy, starring Simon Cadell

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth and Richard finally move into their new country retreat, and while she thinks it is 'olde worlde bijou', he finds it a decided disappointment. Suburban sitcom, starring Patricia Routledge, Clive Swift and Geoffrey Hughes

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Howard finds himself with some explaining to do after Pearl discovers an incriminating long blonde hair on his person, so he turns to his friends for help. Peter Sallis stars

programme.title: Dalziel & Pascoe
programme.desc: The duo are sent to investigate the discovery of a skeleton in an old Yorkshire mining town. When they are told the remains are those of a police officer who worked undercover during the miners' strike in the mid-1980s, the mismatched coppers begin to unravel a complex web of crimes. However, just when they think they are making progress, the killer strikes again

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Gerry and the team agree to help a man who believes his wife is still alive, even though she apparently died 18 months earlier in a car accident. They decide the best way to convince him is to have her exhumed - only to be faced with more questions than answers when it turns out the body is not hers after all. Meanwhile, Brian needs to find a handyman when Esther bans him from his latest DIY project. Hugh Dennis and Samuel West guest star, with Amanda Redman

programme.title: Taggart
programme.desc: Newly promoted DI Reid leads the murder investigation when a busker is stabbed to death down a dark alley in Glasgow city centre. Vital evidence turns up in the hands of two art students who were photographing the area at the time, but as the case progresses, Ross's personal life begins to unravel. Detective drama, starring Blythe Duff, John Michie and Alex Norton, with Siobhan Redmond, Niall Greig Fulton and Steven Pacey

programme.title: The Heart Guy
programme.desc: When he learns the identity of the new owner, Hugh tries to stop the farm sale, Matt is torn between his feelings for Charlie and April, and Meryl uncovers council corruption

programme.title: The Heart Guy
programme.desc: Hugh's world is turned upside down after Harriet gives birth to their baby, while Meryl and Ajax clash over his plans for a cemetery on the family farm

programme.title: Life on Mars
programme.desc: Sam and the rest of the CID unit get to work in an effort to prove Gene's innocence when he becomes a suspect in a murder case. But as the investigation continues, further evidence is stacked up against Hunt, leading to a conclusion that rocks the very foundations of the detective's already fragile world. John Simm and Philip Glenister star

programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part two of two. Jo bonds with a teenager being groomed by the traffickers and Mickey leads a sting to catch the ringleader of the operation. programme.desc: Eddi struggles with the repercussions of the previous night's New Year's celebrations, and clashes with Luc over the correct course of treatment for a hung-over woman she found unconscious and out in the cold. Meanwhile, Sahira worries she might have made a mistake by asking a patient to hypnotise Greg, and Ollie is grateful for his good working relationship with Dan, but begins to miss the high-octane work he experienced in cardio-thoracic surgery

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Tess vents her fury at Abs when Sam fails to make it home from a night out with his colleagues. Guppy is irritated when he finds Stitch has been up all night gambling on the internet, and Dixie tries to cause friction between Cyd and Greg. Peter O'Brien, Elyes Gabel and Suzanne Packer star

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Stevie helps Stuart uncover a burglary scam operating from a Santa's grotto run by an ex-con, and a few heads are turned when Father Christmas is brought in for questioning. The trail of evidence leads the team via a loan shark and fake passport dealer to a crime kingpin who is planning to skip the country. He is a man Stevie knows from when she worked undercover, and is convinced he is up to something big

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: The Mitchells and their guests celebrate Grant and Tiffany's marriage blessing. Bianca finds out a secret.

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: It's Bank Holiday Monday and Ricky's nervous about his first professional speedway race. Meanwhile, Bianca's worried she might be pregnant

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: Elizabeth returns from hospital and is unimpressed with the way Regina is behaving towards George and asks her to leave, and Olivia and James decide that the best chance for their family lies in the city. Romantic drama, starring Noni Hazlehurst

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: Tristan takes a shine to the owner of a shy tortoise, and Mrs Donovan's home-made animal remedies cause more problems than they cure. Rural drama, starring Christopher Timothy, Robert Hardy and Peter Davison

programme.title: Dangerfield
programme.desc: Joanna discovers Paul has a secret love and as emotions run high the couple are forced to make a choice

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: In an effort to find out what life is like for Darryl behind bars, Tracey decides to build her own prison in the attic - with Sharon as her cellmate. Comedy, with Linda Robson and Pauline Quirke

programme.title: Hi-de-Hi!
programme.desc: Ted has a chance of stardom when he is hired as a late replacement at an uptown show for a fellow comedian. It is rumoured that talent scouts from the TV show Opportunity Knocks will be in the audience. Classic comedy following life at Maplin's holiday camp, starring Paul Shane, Su Pollard and Ruth Madoc

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth press-gangs a reluctant Richard into helping repair the lights at the local church hall. Comedy, starring Patricia Routledge and Clive Swift

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: A solicitor's letter addressed to Compo causes a stir in the town - but terrifies his son Tom. Comedy, starring Peter Sallis and Tom Owen

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: Mallory and Father Brown go missing, and it is up to the gang to save them from a man hell-bent on revenge. Mark Williams and Jack Deam star in the crime drama

programme.title: Happy Valley
programme.desc: Things aren't looking good for the kidnappers, with Ashley under witness protection and Kevin awaiting trial. Clare's plan to cheer Catherine up with a birthday party fails miserably when the family ends up arguing, with Daniel revealing a few home truths. What none of them realise is that Tommy is back in Hebden Bridge, weakened by his injuries and hiding out in an abandoned narrowboat, where he starts plotting to see his son. Sarah Lancashire, Siobhan Finneran, James Norton and Karl Davies star

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The squad reopens the investigation into an actress's disappearance when clips of her being assaulted in a 1990 movie are posted on the internet. Director Don Maddox features in the footage, but when questioned, he appears to have problems distinguishing fact from fiction. A potential witness is then found dead, and Brian and Jack are seconded to the unit handling the case, only to find Emily Driscoll (Dennis Waterman's daughter Hannah) is heading the operation. Imogen Stubbs and Sandra Dickinson also guest star

programme.title: Trial & Retribution
programme.desc: Part one of two. A massive police search leads to the body of a missing five-year-old, the victim of sexual assault and strangulation. All the evidence points to a local drunkard being the guilty party _ but as the case goes to trial it becomes clear that the outcome is by no means assured. Gripping crime drama by Lynda La Plante, starring David Hayman, Kate Buffery, Helen McCrory and Rhys Ifans

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Stevie helps Stuart uncover a burglary scam operating from a Santa's grotto run by an ex-con, and a few heads are turned when Father Christmas is brought in for questioning. The trail of evidence leads the team via a loan shark and fake passport dealer to a crime kingpin who is planning to skip the country. He is a man Stevie knows from when she worked undercover, and is convinced he is up to something big

programme.title: MOTD Live: UEFA Euro 2020
programme.desc: Finland v Russia (Kick-off 2.00pm). Mark Chapman presents coverage of the Group B fixture, which takes place at Saint Petersburg Stadium. These countries have only met on four previous occasions, most recently in June 2009, when Russia recorded a straightforward 3-0 victory in a World Cup qualifier in Helsinki. Finland won their opener against Denmark, in a match that was completely overshadowed by the collapse on the field by Christian Eriksen, while Russia were thrashed 3-0 by Belgium on Saturday night. Analysis is provided by Alex Scott and Micah Richards, while commentary comes from Robyn Cowen and Martin Keown

programme.title: MOTD Live: UEFA Euro 2020
programme.desc: Turkey v Wales (Kick-off 5.00pm). Gabby Logan presents all the action from the Group A encounter, which takes place at Olympic Stadium in Baku, Azerbaijan. Turkey were thrashed 3-0 by an impressive Italian side in the opening match of the tournament last Friday, and need to quickly regain their composure and confidence if they're to have any chance of qualifying for the last 16. Wales were also poor in their first match, but managed to secure a crucial 1-1 draw against Switzerland. These two countries have never met in the finals of a major tournament before, and their most recent match took place way back in August 1997. A memorable World Cup qualifier in Istanbul finished 6-4 to the home side, with legendary striker Hakan Sukur bagging four of Turkey's goals. Mark Hughes and Ashley Williams provide analysis, while commentary comes from Steve Wilson and Robbie Savage

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Tiffany calls a beautician to help prepare for her photo shoot, and keen to look her best, she makes a snap decision. Callum feels helpless about Fitzy, so Whitney promises to help him, Suki gets on the wrong side of Vi, and Nancy and Frankie meddle in Shirley's love life. Jean has some advice for Ruby, who feels like giving up

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Things are looking up for Kat when she gets the keys to the launderette and Phil sorts out a taxi licence, until a comment from Sharon leaves her feeling uncertain. Ben is suspicious when he spots Callum and Whitney getting into a cab together, Tiffany tries to cover her mistake and Rocky pulls out the stops to apologise to Sonia

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: Joe Lycett presents the final, in which judges Esme Young and Patrick Grant challenge the three remaining sewers' advanced skills. First up they have to make a little girl's bridesmaid dress, complete with puff sleeves, lined bodice and big bows. The transformation round sees them turn cushions, curtains and tablecloths into dramatic and fun summer festival outfits, before they construct an evening gown worthy of the red carpet in the most important made-to-measure of the competition

programme.title: UEFA Euro 2020 Highlights
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents action from the day's three games - Turkey v Wales and Italy v Switzerland in Group A, and Finland v Russia in Group B. Wales made the semi-finals of Euro 2016 despite losing their second group fixture to England. The Welsh had not beaten Turkey since 1981, while the last meeting - a World Cup qualifier in 1997 - resulted in a 6-4 win for the Turks in Istanbul

programme.title: Roberto Martinez: Whistle to Whistle
programme.desc: Cameras follow the Belgium national team manager Roberto Martinez as he prepares for Euro 2020. The documentary provides behind-the-scenes access to Martinez as he carries out his dual role as head coach and technical director of the number one ranked team in the world

programme.title: MOTD: UEFA Euro 2020 Replay
programme.desc: Turkey v Wales. A chance to see the second Group A fixture for both sides, which took place at Olympic Stadium in Baku, Azerbaijan. Commentary from Steve Wilson and Robbie Savage

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Tommy Walsh visits a Norfolk house where someone had already done half the renovation work before selling it at the auction. Dion Dublin is in Leicestershire, and playing detective to find out where some drips are coming from, while Martin Roberts has fun wielding a big stick at a dated dwelling in Derbyshire

programme.title: Rip Off Britain: Holidays
programme.desc: The holiday-makers left footing the bill after insurers refused to cough up for their medical emergencies, the travellers sent to stay in hotels that they found out didn't actually exist. Plus, how how a legal expert helped one couple escape from a timeshare contract, as well as getting back nearly £8,000 paid to a rogue law firm

programme.title: The Moment of Proof
programme.desc: How a satnav revealed the mastermind behind the biggest armed robbery ever to hit Devon and Cornwall, and a hoodie helped police take a dangerous attacker off the streets

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: At the British Motor Museum in Warwick, two teams scour a fair in the hope that they will find items to make them a profit at auction. Providing a helping hand are experts Charles Hanson and Danny Sebastian, while presenter Natasha Raskin Sharp oversees the proceedings

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Sid is nervous about his presentation, Valerie comes to a startling decision and Luca treats a teenager whose attempts to help her brother have disastrously backfired

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore rescues a broken-up sideboard, two rattan chairs and wooden bed ends from the recycling centre, hoping she and her expert friends can turn them into money-making items

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin heads to rural Gloucestershire to help a house-hunting couple of empty-nesters, who hope their £700,000 budget will secure a slice of country village life. While in the area, he also visits the Cotswold Gliding Club and takes to the skies in a glider, giving him a thrilling bird's-eye view of the countryside below

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Recently married couple Scott and Lisa call on David and Harry Rich and Arit Anderson to bring an Alpine-cum-Oktoberfest element to their garden in Chesham, Buckinghamshire, thus giving Munich-born wife Lisa a taste of home. The brief is exciting, but the budget isn't - it's just £3,000

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: The dealers are intrigued by a vintage petrol lighter based on Donald Campbell's land speed record racing car, a Georgian needlework box, a 1970s kitsch musical drinks decanter and a collection of Second World War memorabilia. Presented by Nigel Havers

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Ronan Keating present the live magazine programme featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: MOTD Live: UEFA Euro 2020
programme.desc: Netherlands v Austria (Kick-off 8.00pm). Gary Lineker presents coverage of both sides' second Group C fixture, which takes place at Johan Cruijff ArenA in Amsterdam. The Dutch hold an excellent record against their opponents this evening, having won their previous six meetings, a run stretching all the way back to 1992. The most recent of those triumphs came in June 2016, when Vincent Janssen and Georginio Wijnaldum found the net in a routine 2-0 friendly victory for Oranje at Ernst Happel Stadion in Vienna. Austria began their campaign in Bucharest with a 3-1 victory against North Macedonia, and will return there for their final fixture against Ukraine, while the Netherlands have the luxury of remaining in Amsterdam for all of their group fixtures, and began with a 3-2 win in a thriller against Ukraine. Analysis comes from Alan Shearer and Rio Ferdinand, with Jonathan Pearce and Jermaine Jenas on commentary

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce introduces the weekly topical debate, with a panel of politicians and other guests facing questions from an audience made up of members of the general public

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: Adam Fleming, Laura Kuenssberg and Chris Mason present their weekly round-up from Westminster, delivering their usual mix of serious analysis and light-hearted gossip about the biggest stories in politics

programme.title: MOTD: UEFA Euro 2020 Replay
programme.desc: Denmark v Belgium. A chance to see the second Group B fixture for both sides, which took place at Parken in Copenhagen. With commentary from Guy Mowbray and Dion Dublin

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell checks out a three-bedroom semi in Alvaston, Derby, and a basement flat in Blackpool. Dion Dublin meets the developer who bought a Liverpool terrace

programme.title: Rip Off Britain: Holidays
programme.desc: The music fans stuck with tickets for concerts abroad that were cancelled due to coronavirus, and the static caravan owners whose holiday havens are headed for the junkyard

programme.title: The Moment of Proof
programme.desc: How a distinctive deer stalker hat was the red flag to cracking a web of stolen bank cards. Plus, how one careless text message put a dangerous stalker behind bars for the longest sentence in a police force's history. Narrated by Chuku Modu

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles takes a tour across the UK looking at some of the most memorable glass items bought by competing teams, before seeing how they fared at auction. He also tries his hand at glass cutting and shares his tips when it comes to buying and selling glass. Plus, Natasha Raskin Sharp blows some glass and Roo Irvine discusses her passion for glass with Charlie Ross

programme.title: MOTD Live: UEFA Euro 2020
programme.desc: Sweden v Slovakia (Kick-off 2.00pm). Gabby Logan presents coverage of the second Group E match for both sides, which comes from Saint Petersburg Stadium. These countries have only previously played each other five times, and this is their first meeting at a major tournament finals. Both teams got off to encouraging starts, with Sweden holding Spain to a hard-fought goalless draw despite seeing little of the ball, while the Slovakians earned a 2-1 victory over Poland, with Inter Milan defender Milan Skriniar scoring the winner. Analysis comes from Micah Richards, Alex Scott and Ashley Williams, while Robyn Cowen is the commentator

programme.title: Match of the Day Top 10
programme.desc: Gary Lineker, Alan Shearer and Micah Richards discuss the best goals ever scored at the European Championships

programme.title: MOTD Live: UEFA Euro 2020
programme.desc: Croatia v Czech Republic (Kick-off 5.00pm). Gary Lineker presents coverage of the Group D fixture, which takes place at Hampden Park. Both Gareth Southgate and Steve Clarke will be keeping a close eye on this game, as the result here could have major ramifications for England and Scotland's qualification hopes, with the Czechs looking to build on their victory over Scotland, and Croatia aiming to register their first point after losing to England. The most recent game between the sides - and only the third in history - came during the group stages of Euro 2016 in France. Crowd trouble and flares thrown onto the pitch marred the occasion, which ended in a 2-2 draw in Saint-Etienne. Ivan Perisic and Ivan Rakitic had given Croatia a 2-0 lead, but the Czechs hit back through Milan Skoda's header, before Tomas Necid smashed in a penalty in the 93rd minute to snatch a point. Analysis comes from Rio Ferdinand and Jurgen Klinsmann, with Steve Bower and Dion Dublin on commentary

programme.title: Still Open All Hours
programme.desc: Gastric spends the night at Madge's house, but has he found the path to her best bedroom? Granville takes delivery of a bargain consignment from Spain, not knowing exactly what is in the very large crate. Eric and Cyril have a fall out over a lost compass, while Mr Newbold falls in and gets a soaking on a boat trip with Mrs Rossi. The canny grocer has his work cut out getting friends to make up and getting a wet customer home without being spotted by 'Black Widow' Mrs Featherstone. Comedy, starring David Jason, Stephanie Cole, Tim Healy, Johnny Vegas and Sue Holderness

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Tom Allen, Geoff Norcott, Vicki Pepperdine and Esme Young join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As usual, the participants set out to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible fibs about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory, through rounds including Home Truths and Quick-fire Lies

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: Kris Marshall is back as the British detective working on the Caribbean island of Saint Marie, where the sun shines all day and it seems half the residents are killers-in-waiting. And so it appears with the first case when a plantation owner ends up dead during a seance - although with the door firmly closed at the time and all the participants holding hands round the table, who did the deed? The victim's family are convinced it was the work of a ghost said to haunt the plantation, but Humphrey is determined to find a more rational explanation. Meanwhile, Fidel has moved on, leaving Officer Dwayne Myers holding the fort - and while he is struggling to cope, the last thing he needs is the Commissioner stepping in to help. Crime drama, with guest appearances from Holby City's Don Gilet and former Corrie bad girl Natalie Gumede

programme.title: Crouchy's Year Late Euros: Live
programme.desc: Peter Crouch, Maya Jama and Alex Horne delve in to at all the big Euro 2020 matches, including reaction to Scotland v England. They are joined by guests Jamie Redknapp, Anne-Marie and Mo Gilligan for some lively banter and there's footage of Crouchy's time spent with some of the Home Nations players before they headed tournament

programme.title: UEFA Euro 2020 Highlights
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents action from the day's three games - England v Scotland and Croatia v Czech Republic in Group D, and Sweden v Slovakia in Group E. The last time the auld enemies met at a major tournament, Paul Gascoigne scored a famous volley as England went on to make the semi-finals of Euro 96 and Scotland narrowly exited the group stage on goals scored. Scotland's Wembley victory in 1999 is their only away win in this fixture in 40 years. With analysis from Micah Richards and Charlie Adam

programme.title: MOTD: UEFA Euro 2020 Replay
programme.desc: England v Scotland. A chance to see the Group D fixture between the old rivals, which came from Wembley Stadium. England had won eight of the 11 most recent meetings, including a famous victory on home soil at Euro 96, while Scotland were bidding for only a second away triumph in this fixture since 1981. With commentary from Guy Mowbray, Martin Keown and James McFadden

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross presents the show from Ardingly antiques fair, and finds out about a distinctive style of wood carving. Experts Philip Serrell and Roo Irvine assist the teams

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Jason Manford hosts the quiz in which contestants aim to find the single 'unbeatable' answer and streak ahead of the rest. Battling it out today are Liam, Sam, Sarah and Kiya

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: Theo Paphitis makes a temporary comeback in the Den, standing in for Peter Jones who is self-isolating. A father-and-daughter duo demonstrate their airbag safety equipment, and a self-balancing serving tray is put through its paces by a hands-on Theo. The Dragons are bewildered by a multitudinous mix of merchandise on offer by a planet-saving entrepreneur, and finally an Irish couple pitch their anti-roll changing mat for babies

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international stories as they break

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Tennis: Queen's
programme.desc: The cinch Championships. Sue Barker presents coverage of day three of the men's grass-court tournament at Queen's Club, featuring the opening second-round singles matches. The first four matches will be expected to take place today, with the concluding four tomorrow, and the players in action will hope to follow in the footsteps of some unlikely victors at this stage in 2019. Unseeded players Diego Schwartzman and Gilles Simon, qualifier Nicolas Mahut, and wildcard entrant Feliciano Lopez all managed to progress to the last eight, with Lopez benefiting from a walkover victory when Juan Martin del Potro had to withdraw without taking to the court

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Anita Rani, Chris Ramsey, Janet Ellis and Jamie Theakston test their general knowledge skills in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games this week

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels from the chateaux of the Loire Valley to the heart of the Champagne region at Reims. Beginning his journey in historic Orleans, Michael follows his Bradshaw's guide to the magnificent stained-glass windows of the Cathedral of Sainte-Croix, before taking a spin around the track at Le Mans in a French-built car. Finally in Versailles, Michael visits the opulent palace and neighbouring Trianon Palace hotel

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Dr Ranj Singh and Dr Zoe Williams go looking for antiques in Lancashire and Greater Manchester, aided by experts Stephanie Connell and Tim Medhurst. Ranj visits a Blackpool dance hall and Zoe joins the crew of a steam train before attending an auction in Harrogate, where they hope to make a profit on their purchases

programme.title: Remarkable Places to Eat
programme.desc: The return of the programme in which leading food experts take Fred Sirieix to their favourite restaurants to explore what makes them so special. Here, Fred heads to Marrakesh in the company of TV chef Andi Oliver, whose love of soul food has led to an obsession with the city's rich culinary heritage. The maitre d' reveals he has been to Marrakesh before but has yet to have a truly memorable meal. Can Andi change his mind as they are served lamb and couscous at a legendary family restaurant, chicken tagine for under £10, and breakfast on a spectacular roof terrace?

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: As news of the Covid-19 virus broke around the world, teams of scientists jumped into action to create a vaccine against a virus that no one had heard of before. This programme looks at the extraordinary inside story of the unprecedented quest to develop vaccines to fight Covid-19, with unique access to five teams of scientists around the globe. Narrated by Lesley Sharp

programme.title: Amazing Hotels: Life Beyond the Lobby
programme.desc: Bite-sized edition of the documentary, which sees Giles Coren and Monica Galetti working alongside the staff of Marina Bay Sands hotel in Singapore

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events

programme.title: Hospital
programme.desc: University Hospitals Coventry and Warwickshire NHS Trust assess the aftermath of the pandemic and its long-term impact, and with the plastic surgery department finally able to start elective operations again, difficult decisions must be made over the investment in robotic surgery

programme.title: Beechgrove
programme.desc: Kirsty Wilson takes a tour of Spring's finest in the Royal Botanic Gardens, Edinburgh, showcasing riveting rhododendrons and majestic meconopsis. Carole Baxter and Mairi Rattray get stuck into Beechgrove's bog and pond areas, while in sunny Joppa, George Anderson continues to show how to garden in small spaces with his summer bedding in pots

programme.title: Inside Culture with Mary Beard
programme.desc: How age-blind casting, such as Ian McKellen in a new production of Hamlet, can change the way we perceive creative work. Lionel Shriver, whose new novel is about a married medical couple contemplating ending their lives in old age, discusses why her characters change their minds as they get older. And Mary Beard finds out why former Coronation Street star Julie Hesmondhalgh has written a play she can perform for the rest of her life

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo heads for the Hertfordshire village of Perry Green to learn about Henry Moore became one of the defining artists of British modernism. In Cambridge, he revisits his former university to hear about a treacherous time in its past

programme.title: Springwatch
programme.desc: Chris Packham and Michaela Strachan report live from Norfolk, and Iolo Williams heads up into the Scottish Highlands to spot some flamboyant avian show-offs. Meanwhile, Gillian Burke looks at some clever cuttlefish in Northern Ireland

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin is delighted with the quirky original features he finds in a house in Staffordshire, and Tommy Walsh meets a sports insurer who is keen to try out his DIY skills on an end-of-terrace in Suffolk. Martin Roberts, meanwhile, is surprised by the extra benefits that come with a pair of old garages in South London

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Jason Manford hosts the quiz in which contestants aim to find the single 'unbeatable' answer and streak ahead of the rest. Battling it out today are Sam, Sharna, Gary and Sandy

programme.title: Your Home Made Perfect
programme.desc: Architects Will Foster and Lynsey Elliott compete to remodel a home in Gosport, Hampshire, that holds 20 years of family memories and a lifetime of antiques and heirlooms for soon-to-be grandparents Lisa and Judy. Angela Scanlon presents

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international stories as they break

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Tennis: Queen's
programme.desc: The cinch Championships. Sue Barker presents coverage of day four of the men's grass-court tournament at Queen's Club, featuring the concluding second-round singles matches. Four of the eight seeded players failed to progress from this stage when the tournament was last held two years ago, and while Juan Martin del Potro was unfortunate to have to withdraw through injury, Kevin Anderson, Marin Cilic, and Stan Wawrinka all suffered defeat on the hallowed turf

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Comedian Chris Ramsey and broadcasters Anita Rani, Janet Ellis and Jamie Theakston continue the daily test of their general knowledge skills. Hosted by Richard Osman

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels across Paris, learning about the avant-garde artists of Montparnasse, going backstage at Folies Bergere and exploring the cellars of Champagne country

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Actor Neil Morrissey and his friend the TV presenter JJ Chalmers search for bargains in Cheshire and Staffordshire, with the help of Izzie Balmer and Charles Hanson. Along the way, Neil has a go at some elaborate ceramic decoration, while JJ gets dressed up as a cow and joins a troupe of dancing villagers

programme.title: The Great Northern Garden Build
programme.desc: Documentary following the creation of RHS Garden Bridgewater in Salford, one of the most ambitious and challenging gardening projects the UK has ever seen. With the major construction work well under way, what impact will a global pandemic have on the build, and will the garden be ready in time for its grand opening?

programme.title: Together (2021)
programme.desc: A husband and wife are forced to re-evaluate themselves and their relationship through the reality of lockdown. She is a charity worker, a co-ordinator for all of Europe at a refugee charity. She's the daughter of a dentist father and an 'old socialist' mother - the only grandparent still around. He, meanwhile, is a self-employed, self-made man who runs a boutique computing consultancy. He's been forced to furlough his staff and take up growing vegetables - something he's very proud of. Drama, starring James McAvoy and Sharon Horgan

programme.title: Amazing Hotels: Life Beyond the Lobby
programme.desc: Giles Coren and Monica Galetti head to the Andean Cloud Forest of Ecuador to work at Mashpi Lodge, a $10 million modernist hotel featuring an extraordinary gondola cable car that 'flies' guests one mile through the jungle canopy at a dizzying height

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Dara O Briain and Hugh Dennis pan through the last week and extract nuggets of comedy gold, with guests Maisie Adam, Angela Barnes, Ed Byrne, Glenn Moore and Evelyn Mok

programme.title: Botham: The Legend of '81
programme.desc: A look back at Ian Botham's cricketing career, originally shown in 2011 to mark 30 years since the epic Ashes series that saw the former all-rounder at the peak of his powers. Botham started the series against Australia in 1981 as England captain, but resigned following a string of poor performances and media criticism, and what followed at Headingley, Edgbaston and Old Trafford led to the series becoming remembered as Botham's Ashes. This programme charts the player's highs and lows on and off the pitch, as well as focusing on his epic fundraising achievements and subsequent broadcasting career. With contributions from David Gower, Viv Richards, Mick Jagger and Stephen Fry

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: Joe Lycett presents as the contestants create garments inspired by the movies, beginning with a pattern challenge to make Baby's iconic dress from Dirty Dancing. For the transformation round, the judges take their inspiration from Julie Andrews in The Sound of Music, asking the sewers to turn old curtains into children's clothes, while the made-to-measure task sees them creating dazzling 1970s-influenced disco dresses, as worn by the Dreamgirls

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo is in Suffolk on the last leg of his 1930s Bradshaw's inspired tour of East Anglia, visiting the racecourse at Newmarket and the Roman Catholic shrine at Walsingham

programme.title: Springwatch
programme.desc: Insect fan Penny Metal introduces viewers to the tiny residents in a London park, and Iolo Williams checks out some alien species on the forest floor at Alladale Wilderness Reserve. Meanwhile, Gillian Burke looks at spring from a swift's point of view at WWT Castle Espie

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Tommy Walsh visits a Norfolk house where someone had already done half the renovation work before selling it at the auction. Dion Dublin is in Leicestershire, and playing detective to find out where some drips are coming from, while Martin Roberts has fun wielding a big stick at a dated dwelling in Derbyshire

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Over three rounds, contestants Jason, Mark, Kiran and Angela vie to find the single 'unbeatable' answer and streak ahead of the rest to make the final where they can win a cash prize. General knowledge quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.title: Between the Covers
programme.desc: Sara Cox is joined by Don Warrington, Zoe Lyons, Hugh Dennis and Giovanna Fletcher, who discuss Agent Running in the Field by John le Carré and Another Life by Jodie Chapman, and also share their own favourite books of all time

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo visits Crewe, a town steeped in railway history, to investigate the making of cinema classic The Night Mail during the 1930s. He then heads to Chester to discover the interwar origins of Britain's most popular zoo, then goes across the border to the Welsh village of Gresford, scene of one of Britain's worst mining disasters

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international stories as they break

programme.title: Tennis: Queen's
programme.desc: The cinch Championships. Sue Barker presents coverage of day five of the men's grass-court tournament at Queen's Club, featuring the singles quarter-finals. The corresponding stage of the tournament staged here in 2019 produced an upset to delight the crowd, with wildcard entrant Feliciano Lopez securing a 4-6, 6-4, 7-6 triumph over sixth seed Milos Raonic. Elsewhere, their were also victories for Felix Auger-Aliassime, Daniil Medvedev, and Gilles Simon over Stefanos Tsitsipas, Diego Schwartzman, and Nicolas Mahut respectively

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Anita Rani, Chris Ramsey, Janet Ellis and Jamie Theakston compete in Double Points Friday, and their scores are tallied from the week resulting in an overall champion being crowned

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo embarks on a journey through Germany, steered by a Bradshaw's guide. His unique window on Europe between the wars takes him through a dark period in German history, when the nation's first democracy and its vibrant culture of art, design and decadence were swept away by fascism, nationalism and the increasing likelihood of war

programme.title: Our Lives
programme.desc: Documentary following two men preparing to compete in the World Stone Skimming Championships, held on the tiny west coast island of Easdale in Scotland. Dougie is eight-time World Stone Skimming champion and native Scot from Blairgowrie, and although he is confident, newcomer Alex threatens his legendary status. The programme charts the determination and dedication of the competitors, and reveals what it truly takes to come out on top in this unique contest

programme.title: Inside Culture with Mary Beard
programme.desc: In a special programme, Mary Beard is in conversation with critically acclaimed and internationally bestselling Nigerian author Chimamanda Ngozi Adichie. Her latest book, Notes on Grief, conveys her startlingly powerful reaction to losing her father last year, the man she credits with instilling in her a strong feminist instinct. Her writing is both tender and fierce, whether it's her Women's Prize-winning novel Half of a Yellow Sun or her influential essay We Should All Be Feminists, and she's often named as one of Africa's best writers. In her first public appearance since the news of her mother's death, she talks to Mary about the power of literature to heal, how it feels to have Beyonce using her words in her songs and why Enid Blyton was her inspiration to write as she was growing up in Nigeria

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: As midsummer approaches, it's the perfect time for Monty Don to place succulents outside at Longmeadow, as well as planting out dahlias and tender vegetables. Carol Klein visits Trebah Garden in Cornwall, where she finds plants which can add early summer pazazz to borders. Nick Bailey is at RHS Wisley to look at a variety of shrubs that can be used as alternatives to traditional box hedging

programme.title: A Pandemic Poem: Where Did the World Go?
programme.desc: An examination of the pandemic, using a poem written by Poet Laureate Simon Armitage that charts the progression of Covid-19 from the early rumours arriving from Wuhan to the rollout of the vaccination programme and the easing of lockdown

programme.title: Later - with Jools Holland
programme.desc: Jools is joined by rapper and songwriter Kano, fresh from his performance at Glastonbury Festival's Live at Worthy Farm Event. He chats to Jools about his latest projects, and chooses his favourite moments from the Later archives. There are also debut appearances on the show by Self Esteem and Joel Culpepper

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events

programme.title: Spotlight (2015)
programme.desc: A team of investigative reporters for a Boston newspaper look into claims of children being molested within the Catholic church. The journalists discover the situation is far worse than it seemed, as they find a conspiracy among the city's legal, religious and government establishments to cover up decades of abuse. Oscar-winning fact-based drama, starring Mark Ruffalo, Michael Keaton, Rachel McAdams and Stanley Tucci

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: Part one of three. The search continues for Hera, who is being held by Cavil and the Cylon rebels, and the fleet's members are forced to choose between taking the mission or staying behind in relative safety. With Galactica nearing the end of its life, Adama leads the charge, accompanied by a faithful handful of followers who believe Hera is their only hope for the future

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: Part two of three. With Earth, once hoped to be a refuge for the survivors of the 12 colonies, revealed as a nuclear wasteland, and the deterioration of the Galactica itself, the future of the human race is uncertain. Adama leads a small force of loyalists to rescue half-human-half-Cylon child Hera from the clutches of Cavil and his Cylon faction

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: Part three of three. Last-ever episode of the epic sci-fi series. Gaius and Caprica have a startling revelation, while Roslin struggles to cope with the sights she has seen as the destiny of the humans and Cylons seems to be more fragile than ever. Starring Edward James Olmos

programme.title: Dom Digs In
programme.desc: Dom Littlewood takes a walk on the wild side, working as a zookeeper at the largest zoo in the UK. As he mucks in, he discovers that the zoo's conservation efforts are helping make sure that many of these endangered species survive in the wild for generations to come

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Return to Pembrokeshire Farm
programme.desc: Griff Rhys Jones's project nears completion - until he learns there is trouble with the roofs of the mill and the cottage. George travels to one of the last working watermills in Wales to get ideas for the mill's interior

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross paints a landscape of a clear windless day, where the reflections in the water are so smooth they appear to be crystal likenesses of the mountains above

programme.title: Timeshift
programme.desc: The history of the steamers which were once a common sight on rivers and coastal waters around Britain and were a highlight of seaside holidays from the 1820s until the early 1960s, providing the adventure and trappings of an ocean voyage without venturing out of sight of land

programme.title: Titanic's Tragic Twin: The Britannic Disaster
programme.desc: Kate Humble and Andy Torbet try to piece together the events of November 21, 1916, when the Britannic, sister ship to the Titanic, sank in just 55 minutes. Kate speaks to some of the survivors' descendants, including those of Violet Jessops, who survived both Titanic and Britannic, and look-out Archie Jewell, who survived when those around him lost their lives

programme.title: BBC Cardiff Singer of the World
programme.desc: Petroc Trelawny hosts the fourth round of the contest from St David's Hall, Cardiff, in which young singers from around the world perform with the Welsh National Opera Orchestra, conducted by Michael Christie. Neal Davies, Roberta Alexander and Aidan Lang judge their efforts

programme.title: Great British Photography Challenge
programme.desc: The photographers face three final assignments, including a wildcard challenge where they are given free rein to try and get one last extraordinary shot for their portfolios. They are also in Glencoe for a dramatic landscape challenge and must curate and mount what they hope will be a winning exhibition for the judges. Choosing a maximum of 15 images from the hundreds they've taken across the course of the series is a huge task in itself

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross paints a landscape of a clear windless day, where the reflections in the water are so smooth they appear to be crystal likenesses of the mountains above

programme.title: Return to Pembrokeshire Farm
programme.desc: Griff Rhys Jones's project nears completion - until he learns there is trouble with the roofs of the mill and the cottage. George travels to one of the last working watermills in Wales to get ideas for the mill's interior

programme.title: Return to Pembrokeshire Farm
programme.desc: The day of the festival arrives, and the showman's wagon is revealed to the locals. Plus, Griff Rhys Jones reflects on George's first job as an architectural designer as work on the cottage and the mill is finally completed

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross takes a detour from the trail and decides to capture a rarely used path through country woods

programme.title: Florence Foster Jenkins (2016)
programme.desc: A tone deaf socialite imagines she is a great singer, and her conniving yet devoted husband allows her to remain in her fantasy by holding concerts with small, invited audiences. However, reality threatens to intrude when she announces her intention of realising a lifelong dream of performing in New York's Carnegie Hall, recruiting a struggling young pianist to aid her. Fact-based comedy, starring Meryl Streep and Hugh Grant

programme.title: BBC Cardiff Singer of the World
programme.desc: Petroc Trelawny introduces the final of the BBC Cardiff Singer of the World Song Prize 2021 from St David's Hall. With judges Neal Davies, Roberta Alexander and Aidan Lang. Featuring the Welsh National Opera Orchestra under conductor Michael Christie

programme.title: The Sky at Night
programme.desc: Chris Lintott and the team look at companies spearheading the boom in Britain's space industry. Plus, Pete Lawrence discusses ice crystals in the upper atmosphere lit by the sun

programme.title: Arena
programme.desc: Profile of the acclaimed director of Don't Look Now, The Man who Fell to Earth, Walkabout and Performance. Roeg reflects on the recurring themes of his work, including perception of time and the difficulty of human relationships. With contributions from actors Donald Sutherland, Julie Christie, Jenny Agutter and Theresa Russell, and film-makers who have been inspired by his work, including Danny Boyle and Ben Wheatley

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross takes a detour from the trail and decides to capture a rarely used path through country woods

programme.title: Return to Pembrokeshire Farm

programme.title: Timeshift
programme.desc: Documentary examining the use of physically imposed discipline, and how attitudes toward its application have changed over the years. The film investigates the ways schools, religion and the justice system have been involved with corporal punishment, and how its imposition has also provoked debate from a sexual aspect

programme.title: Fathers and Songs: Music for Father's Day
programme.desc: A selection of songs that celebrate fatherhood and probe the emotional complexities of a man's relationship with his son or daughter. Featured artists of past and present include Kid Creole and the Coconuts, Cat Stevens, Emmylou Harris, Paul Simon, Madonna, Peter Gabriel, Mike and the Mechanics, Suggs and the Blockheads, Neil Young, James Brown, Ozzy and Kelly Osbourne and Pigbag

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Jackie Brambles presents the edition first aired on February 28 1991, featuring performances by N-Joi, Massive Attack, Jesus Jones, Free, the Clash, the Source featuring Candi Staton, Madonna and the Simpsons

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Nicky Campbell presents the edition first aired on March 7 1991, featuring performances by Hale & Pace and the Stonkers, Rocky V featuring Joey B Ellis and Tynetta Hare, Quartz introducing Dina Carroll, Living Colour, Ned's Atomic Dustbin, Xpansions, Roxette, the Clash and the Charlatans

programme.title: Pop Charts Britannia: 60 Years of the Top 10
programme.desc: The evolution of feelings and attitudes toward the British singles chart. Documenting changes from the 1952 NME countdown via Pick and Top of the Pops through to the Radio 1 chart show of 2012. With contributions from chart show presenters David Jacobs and Reggie Yates, music fans Grace Dent and Pete Paphides and veteran musicians Jon Webster and Rob Dickins

programme.title: REM at the BBC
programme.desc: A selection of archive performances from throughout the band's career, originally recorded for BBC programmes including The Old Grey Whistle Test, Top of the Pops, Parkinson, The Late Show and Later with Jools Holland. Songs featured include Losing My Religion, Orange Crush, Pretty Persuasion and Half a World Away. Singer Michael Stipe and bass player Mike Mills also reflect on the band's split in September 2011

programme.title: Huey Morgan's Latin Music Adventure
programme.desc: Huey visits New York City, home to over a million Puerto Ricans and the place where he first fell in love with the hot sounds of Latin Music. 'El Barrio' or Spanish Harlem became the focal point for the community, with Latin music the soundtrack to their survival. Fusing traditional music with American R'n'B and soul, newly minted Nuyoricans created the '60s dance craze, the Boogaloo. Flying south to Puerto Rico itself Huey sets off on a journey to discover the music that was left behind, hunting down the roots of Salsa in the capital San Juan, and the traditional folk styles of Bomba and Plena

programme.title: Latin Music: A Session With
programme.desc: A performance by the world renowned Cuatro player, the Latin American guitar made famous in Luis Fonsi's hit song Despacito. The Grammy Award-winning star is joined by family and friends in the mountains of Puerto Rico to present some of his favourite music.

programme.title: Snaps
programme.desc: Would you turn your back on your religion just so you could fit in? 14 year-old Jag is a care-free, easy-going Sikh boy who loves nothing more than playing football with his mates, but he has finally lost his patience with his classmates after the new kid at school asks if he is wearing a handkerchief around his topknot. Jag feels like he spends his life answering stupid questions about his hair and has had enough. Is this something he is going to have to face all his life? And why didn't his friends stick up for him? Would life be simpler if he just cut his hair? After all his brother Kully did just that, and life seems easy for him now...but it's not quite that simple for Jag. In this short coming-of age-film, we see Jag make one of the toughest decisions of his life to date. Does he honour his Sikh family values and traditions and start to wear a turban, or will he cut off his hair to fit in with his mates?

programme.title: MOTD Live: UEFA Euro 2020
programme.desc: Finland v Russia (Kick-off 2.00pm). Mark Chapman presents coverage of the Group B fixture, which takes place at Saint Petersburg Stadium. These countries have only met on four previous occasions, most recently in June 2009, when Russia recorded a straightforward 3-0 victory in a World Cup qualifier in Helsinki. Finland won their opener against Denmark, in a match that was completely overshadowed by the collapse on the field by Christian Eriksen, while Russia were thrashed 3-0 by Belgium on Saturday night. Analysis is provided by Alex Scott and Micah Richards, while commentary comes from Robyn Cowen and Martin Keown

programme.title: MOTD Live: UEFA Euro 2020
programme.desc: Turkey v Wales (Kick-off 5.00pm). Gabby Logan presents all the action from the Group A encounter, which takes place at Olympic Stadium in Baku, Azerbaijan. Turkey were thrashed 3-0 by an impressive Italian side in the opening match of the tournament last Friday, and need to quickly regain their composure and confidence if they're to have any chance of qualifying for the last 16. Wales were also poor in their first match, but managed to secure a crucial 1-1 draw against Switzerland. These two countries have never met in the finals of a major tournament before, and their most recent match took place way back in August 1997. A memorable World Cup qualifier in Istanbul finished 6-4 to the home side, with legendary striker Hakan Sukur bagging four of Turkey's goals. Mark Hughes and Ashley Williams provide analysis, while commentary comes from Steve Wilson and Robbie Savage

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Tiffany calls a beautician to help prepare for her photo shoot, and keen to look her best, she makes a snap decision. Callum feels helpless about Fitzy, so Whitney promises to help him, Suki gets on the wrong side of Vi, and Nancy and Frankie meddle in Shirley's love life. Jean has some advice for Ruby, who feels like giving up

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Things are looking up for Kat when she gets the keys to the launderette and Phil sorts out a taxi licence, until a comment from Sharon leaves her feeling uncertain. Ben is suspicious when he spots Callum and Whitney getting into a cab together, Tiffany tries to cover her mistake and Rocky pulls out the stops to apologise to Sonia

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: Joe Lycett presents the final, in which judges Esme Young and Patrick Grant challenge the three remaining sewers' advanced skills. First up they have to make a little girl's bridesmaid dress, complete with puff sleeves, lined bodice and big bows. The transformation round sees them turn cushions, curtains and tablecloths into dramatic and fun summer festival outfits, before they construct an evening gown worthy of the red carpet in the most important made-to-measure of the competition

programme.title: Elis James' Feast of Football
programme.desc: Elis James and regular guest Danny Gabbidon look back at Wales' second match of the tournament against Turkey

programme.title: UEFA Euro 2020 Highlights
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents action from the day's three games - Turkey v Wales and Italy v Switzerland in Group A, and Finland v Russia in Group B. Wales made the semi-finals of Euro 2016 despite losing their second group fixture to England. The Welsh had not beaten Turkey since 1981, while the last meeting - a World Cup qualifier in 1997 - resulted in a 6-4 win for the Turks in Istanbul

programme.title: MOTD: UEFA Euro 2020 Replay
programme.desc: Turkey v Wales. A chance to see the second Group A fixture for both sides, which took place at Olympic Stadium in Baku, Azerbaijan. Commentary from Steve Wilson and Robbie Savage

programme.desc: Tommy Walsh visits a Norfolk house where someone had already done half the renovation work before selling it at the auction. Dion Dublin is in Leicestershire, and playing detective to find out where some drips are coming from, while Martin Roberts has fun wielding a big stick at a dated dwelling in Derbyshire

programme.title: Rip Off Britain: Holidays
programme.desc: The holiday-makers left footing the bill after insurers refused to cough up for their medical emergencies, the travellers sent to stay in hotels that they found out didn't actually exist. Plus, how how a legal expert helped one couple escape from a timeshare contract, as well as getting back nearly £8,000 paid to a rogue law firm

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin heads to rural Gloucestershire to help a house-hunting couple of empty-nesters, who hope their £700,000 budget will secure a slice of country village life. While in the area, he also visits the Cotswold Gliding Club and takes to the skies in a glider, giving him a thrilling bird's-eye view of the countryside below

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Recently married couple Scott and Lisa call on David and Harry Rich and Arit Anderson to bring an Alpine-cum-Oktoberfest element to their garden in Chesham, Buckinghamshire, thus giving Munich-born wife Lisa a taste of home. The brief is exciting, but the budget isn't - it's just £3,000

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: The dealers are intrigued by a vintage petrol lighter based on Donald Campbell's land speed record racing car, a Georgian needlework box, a 1970s kitsch musical drinks decanter and a collection of Second World War memorabilia. Presented by Nigel Havers

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Ronan Keating present the live magazine programme featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: MOTD Live: UEFA Euro 2020
programme.desc: Netherlands v Austria (Kick-off 8.00pm). Gary Lineker presents coverage of both sides' second Group C fixture, which takes place at Johan Cruijff ArenA in Amsterdam. The Dutch hold an excellent record against their opponents this evening, having won their previous six meetings, a run stretching all the way back to 1992. The most recent of those triumphs came in June 2016, when Vincent Janssen and Georginio Wijnaldum found the net in a routine 2-0 friendly victory for Oranje at Ernst Happel Stadion in Vienna. Austria began their campaign in Bucharest with a 3-1 victory against North Macedonia, and will return there for their final fixture against Ukraine, while the Netherlands have the luxury of remaining in Amsterdam for all of their group fixtures, and began with a 3-2 win in a thriller against Ukraine. Analysis comes from Alan Shearer and Rio Ferdinand, with Jonathan Pearce and Jermaine Jenas on commentary

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce introduces the weekly topical debate, with a panel of politicians and other guests facing questions from an audience made up of members of the general public

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: Adam Fleming, Laura Kuenssberg and Chris Mason present their weekly round-up from Westminster, delivering their usual mix of serious analysis and light-hearted gossip about the biggest stories in politics

programme.title: MOTD: UEFA Euro 2020 Replay
programme.desc: Denmark v Belgium. A chance to see the second Group B fixture for both sides, which took place at Parken in Copenhagen. With commentary from Guy Mowbray and Dion Dublin

programme.title: The Moment of Proof
programme.desc: How a distinctive deer stalker hat was the red flag to cracking a web of stolen bank cards. Plus, how one careless text message put a dangerous stalker behind bars for the longest sentence in a police force's history. Narrated by Chuku Modu

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles takes a tour across the UK looking at some of the most memorable glass items bought by competing teams, before seeing how they fared at auction. He also tries his hand at glass cutting and shares his tips when it comes to buying and selling glass. Plus, Natasha Raskin Sharp blows some glass and Roo Irvine discusses her passion for glass with Charlie Ross

programme.title: BBC Wales Today Coronavirus Update
programme.desc: The latest developments, including the daily Welsh Government news conference

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: MOTD Live: UEFA Euro 2020
programme.desc: Sweden v Slovakia (Kick-off 2.00pm). Gabby Logan presents coverage of the second Group E match for both sides, which comes from Saint Petersburg Stadium. These countries have only previously played each other five times, and this is their first meeting at a major tournament finals. Both teams got off to encouraging starts, with Sweden holding Spain to a hard-fought goalless draw despite seeing little of the ball, while the Slovakians earned a 2-1 victory over Poland, with Inter Milan defender Milan Skriniar scoring the winner. Analysis comes from Micah Richards, Alex Scott and Ashley Williams, while Robyn Cowen is the commentator

programme.title: Match of the Day Top 10
programme.desc: Gary Lineker, Alan Shearer and Micah Richards discuss the best goals ever scored at the European Championships

programme.title: MOTD Live: UEFA Euro 2020
programme.desc: Croatia v Czech Republic (Kick-off 5.00pm). Gary Lineker presents coverage of the Group D fixture, which takes place at Hampden Park. Both Gareth Southgate and Steve Clarke will be keeping a close eye on this game, as the result here could have major ramifications for England and Scotland's qualification hopes, with the Czechs looking to build on their victory over Scotland, and Croatia aiming to register their first point after losing to England. The most recent game between the sides - and only the third in history - came during the group stages of Euro 2016 in France. Crowd trouble and flares thrown onto the pitch marred the occasion, which ended in a 2-2 draw in Saint-Etienne. Ivan Perisic and Ivan Rakitic had given Croatia a 2-0 lead, but the Czechs hit back through Milan Skoda's header, before Tomas Necid smashed in a penalty in the 93rd minute to snatch a point. Analysis comes from Rio Ferdinand and Jurgen Klinsmann, with Steve Bower and Dion Dublin on commentary

programme.title: Still Open All Hours
programme.desc: Gastric spends the night at Madge's house, but has he found the path to her best bedroom? Granville takes delivery of a bargain consignment from Spain, not knowing exactly what is in the very large crate. Eric and Cyril have a fall out over a lost compass, while Mr Newbold falls in and gets a soaking on a boat trip with Mrs Rossi. The canny grocer has his work cut out getting friends to make up and getting a wet customer home without being spotted by 'Black Widow' Mrs Featherstone. Comedy, starring David Jason, Stephanie Cole, Tim Healy, Johnny Vegas and Sue Holderness

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Tom Allen, Geoff Norcott, Vicki Pepperdine and Esme Young join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As usual, the participants set out to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible fibs about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory, through rounds including Home Truths and Quick-fire Lies

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: Kris Marshall is back as the British detective working on the Caribbean island of Saint Marie, where the sun shines all day and it seems half the residents are killers-in-waiting. And so it appears with the first case when a plantation owner ends up dead during a seance - although with the door firmly closed at the time and all the participants holding hands round the table, who did the deed? The victim's family are convinced it was the work of a ghost said to haunt the plantation, but Humphrey is determined to find a more rational explanation. Meanwhile, Fidel has moved on, leaving Officer Dwayne Myers holding the fort - and while he is struggling to cope, the last thing he needs is the Commissioner stepping in to help. Crime drama, with guest appearances from Holby City's Don Gilet and former Corrie bad girl Natalie Gumede

programme.title: Crouchy's Year Late Euros: Live
programme.desc: Peter Crouch, Maya Jama and Alex Horne delve in to at all the big Euro 2020 matches, including reaction to Scotland v England. They are joined by guests Jamie Redknapp, Anne-Marie and Mo Gilligan for some lively banter and there's footage of Crouchy's time spent with some of the Home Nations players before they headed tournament

programme.title: UEFA Euro 2020 Highlights
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents action from the day's three games - England v Scotland and Croatia v Czech Republic in Group D, and Sweden v Slovakia in Group E. The last time the auld enemies met at a major tournament, Paul Gascoigne scored a famous volley as England went on to make the semi-finals of Euro 96 and Scotland narrowly exited the group stage on goals scored. Scotland's Wembley victory in 1999 is their only away win in this fixture in 40 years. With analysis from Micah Richards and Charlie Adam

programme.title: MOTD: UEFA Euro 2020 Replay
programme.desc: England v Scotland. A chance to see the Group D fixture between the old rivals, which came from Wembley Stadium. England had won eight of the 11 most recent meetings, including a famous victory on home soil at Euro 96, while Scotland were bidding for only a second away triumph in this fixture since 1981. With commentary from Guy Mowbray, Martin Keown and James McFadden

These two countries have never met in the finals of a major tournament before, and their most recent match took place way back in August 1997. A memorable World Cup qualifier in Istanbul finished 6-4 to the home side, with legendary striker Hakan Sukur bagging four of Turkey's goals. Mark Hughes and Ashley Williams provide analysis, while commentary comes from Steve Wilson and Robbie Savage

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Tiffany calls a beautician to help prepare for her photo shoot, and keen to look her best, she makes a snap decision. programme.desc: Things are looking up for Kat when she gets the keys to the launderette and Phil sorts out a taxi licence, until a comment from Sharon leaves her feeling uncertain. programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: Joe Lycett presents the final, in which judges Esme Young and Patrick Grant challenge the three remaining sewers' advanced skills. The transformation round sees them turn cushions, curtains and tablecloths into dramatic and fun summer festival outfits, before they construct an evening gown worthy of the red carpet in the most important made-to-measure of the competition

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: The dealers are intrigued by a vintage petrol lighter based on Donald Campbell's land speed record racing car, a Georgian needlework box, a 1970s kitsch musical drinks decanter and a collection of Second World War memorabilia. Presented by Nigel Havers

programme.desc: Alex Jones and Ronan Keating present the live magazine programme featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: MOTD Live: UEFA Euro 2020
programme.desc: Netherlands v Austria (Kick-off 8.00pm). Gary Lineker presents coverage of both sides' second Group C fixture, which takes place at Johan Cruijff ArenA in Amsterdam. The Dutch hold an excellent record against their opponents this evening, having won their previous six meetings, a run stretching all the way back to 1992. The most recent of those triumphs came in June 2016, when Vincent Janssen and Georginio Wijnaldum found the net in a routine 2-0 friendly victory for Oranje at Ernst Happel Stadion in Vienna. Austria began their campaign in Bucharest with a 3-1 victory against North Macedonia, and will return there for their final fixture against Ukraine, while the Netherlands have the luxury of remaining in Amsterdam for all of their group fixtures, and began with a 3-2 win in a thriller against Ukraine. Analysis comes from Alan Shearer and Rio Ferdinand, with Jonathan Pearce and Jermaine Jenas on commentary
