programme.title: Scrubs
programme.desc: The new interns tell the story of how JD won the respect of the staff by standing up to the Chief of Medicine - much to Turk's chagrin. Comedy, starring Zach Braff, Donald Faison, John C McGinley and Sarah Chalke

programme.title: Kirstie's Handmade Treasures
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp visits Manhattan, where she creates candy canes and lollipops at artisan sugar crafter Papabubble, before heading back to the UK to learn how to make passion fruit marshmallows covered in chocolate and coconut

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Retired couple Roger and Obie Pearce want a slice of the Spanish good life. Obie has never been to Spain before, and Roger hasn't visited since he was in the army 40 years ago, but they are determined to relocate to Almeria where Roger's health could benefit from the warm climate. Ben Hillman sets out to find them their perfect home on a budget of £120,000

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Simon O'Brien repurposes some old suitcases, while Henry Cole gives an old dresser some serious bling

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien discover items including stained glass and a Victorian hay rack that are ready for upcycling to turn a profit for their owners

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: Research into family trees uncovers the history of domestic service in one of the world's most prestigious universities and how luxury transatlantic steamliners helped change the course of history. Plus, details of unclaimed estates with heirs still needing to be found

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: A hairdresser has grand plans to expand in the Costa Blanca, and a couple's dream of a new life in France running a yoga retreat falls at the first hurdle

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition starts at Just B in Edinburgh, owned by Karen MacDonald and Geoff Thomson, who hope that their slice of Zen will impress their guests. Nick and Stefan are forced to comply with the house rules in some alternative attire, while Tony and Natalie make some dirty discoveries in their dog-friendly room. At dinner, the hosts serve up their spiritual side, and the competition hots up as Stefan reveals his hospitality pedigree. In the morning, a mushroom misdemeanour leaves a bad taste in Tony's mouth

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit is to Glencoe Villa Guesthouse in Featherstone, West Yorkshire, owned by Ewa Hunt, who is joined by her best friend, Ellie. The homely styled bedrooms aren't to everyone's taste, and at dinner there's a debate about how to host. At breakfast, some guests pick holes in the pre-order system, and in the kitchen Ewa's under pressure

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit of the week takes place at The Fox Inn in Stourton, Staffordshire, owned by uncle and nephew Stefan Carron and Nick Edwards. Most of their guests are bowled over by the bedrooms, but Tony isn't wild about the decor. At dinner, Stefan impresses with some serious celebrity name dropping and Tony reveals his alternative approach to taking on staff. At breakfast, pro chef Nick pulls out all the stops with his presentation, but missing items mean feedback falls short from Tony

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final visit of the week is to The New Inn in Newton, Derbyshire, owned by Tony Carlin, who's joined by his partner Natalie Briggs. On first impressions the guests feel it's lacking luxury, and Ewa and Ellie have cleanliness concerns. At dinner, the luxury label is called into question, and a bust up over the self-service breakfast is narrowly avoided. At breakfast time, the hosts do a disappearing act, which doesn't go down well with their guests

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet for the last time to find out what they've been paid and to settle some scores. Just B is first, and owners Karen and Geoff get some answers while hearing the truth from guests on their hair-raising stay

programme.title: Grand Designs New Zealand
programme.desc: Ric and Boio set out to turn a childhood treehouse dream into reality. But building in a pine forest, which is frequently prone to winter storms, only complicates things

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Keepers introduce elderly chameleon Mr Parsons to two new prospective partners, and bongo Malaeka shows little interest in her potential mate, Moti. Meanwhile in the aquarium, George the pinstripe damba is spotted in distress, and keepers notice a lump on his gill which may be affecting his breathing

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Dick Strawbridge takes children Arthur and Dorothy on a mission to rescue baby toads in their chateau grounds. Donna and Paul put the rest of their renovations on hold as they set themselves the challenge of turning a wing of the chateau into a self-contained apartment for Donna's parents, Anne and Brian

programme.title: Grand Designs: Living in
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud looks at the challenges of self-building in the UK's most remote places, where beautiful landscapes often meet savage weather. Drawing on footage from 15 years of the programme, as well as new visits to extraordinary and incredible homes, Kevin reveals which designs work best, and why. Along the way, he interviews pioneers at the forefront of architecture in the wilderness and sees the astonishing lengths people go to live on the edges of society

programme.title: Building the Dream
programme.desc: Charlie Luxton meets Lloyd and Caroline, who plan to build a sustainable home in Leicestershire. Charlie is worried that their eco-design might cost them the earth, but former soldier Lloyd is determined to run the build like a military operation. However, even his organisational skills cannot ward off a huge crisis with their budget, and what starts out as a passion project soon turns into an endurance test

programme.title: Grand Designs Australia
programme.desc: Marc and Felicity spend years building their dream non-toxic home in Melbourne, only for the unthinkable to happen when fire strikes just before they are about to move in. Will their hopes be shattered for ever?

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Cameras reveal how individuals adapt and survive in the face of adversity. Subjects include Paul, 60, who is rushed to A&E after colliding with a van. Doctors are concerned that he may have serious injuries to his pelvis and spine. There's also a profile of four-year-old Jeremy, who is struggling to breathe. He suffers from a series of chronic conditions, including microcephaly and epilepsy

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the comedy words-and-numbers quiz, as Jon Richardson and Michelle Wolf take on guest captain Jonathan Ross with Johnny Vegas. Three-man comedy act Pappy's joins Countdown's resident lexicographer Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner and Rachel Riley looks after the numbers and letters

programme.title: Building the Dream
programme.desc: Charlie Luxton meets Lloyd and Caroline, who plan to build a sustainable home in Leicestershire. Charlie is worried that their eco-design might cost them the earth, but former soldier Lloyd is determined to run the build like a military operation. However, even his organisational skills cannot ward off a huge crisis with their budget, and what starts out as a passion project soon turns into an endurance test

programme.title: Grand Designs Australia
programme.desc: Marc and Felicity spend years building their dream non-toxic home in Melbourne, only for the unthinkable to happen when fire strikes just before they are about to move in. Will their hopes be shattered for ever?

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Another selection of the team's favourite investigations. Kate Quilton learns how a special enzyme contained in pineapples attacks and destroys dead skin cells in the mouth, with scientists using this enzyme to treat the victims of burns. Jimmy Doherty investigates the catastrophic effects of not getting enough vitamin D when he meets a boy who has rickets, while Matt Tebbutt finds out how a Lincolnshire business produces 1.5 million bottles of elderflower cordial without employing a single flower-picker

programme.title: Kirstie's Handmade Treasures
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp is taught how to bind books by expert Isabelle Ting, and reveals how to create a notebook from leftover materials including leather offcuts, recycled leather coats and decorative paper

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Ben and Kelly Jukes have been saving up for a holiday home on the Costa Blanca since they got married five years ago. With their first baby on the way, they want somewhere where they can soak up the Spanish sun on holidays and that they can share with family and friends. Scarlette Douglas tempts them with five properties in and around Torrevieja for their budget of £55,000

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien travel across the UK to find unique and valuable items, and their latest find is an old bureau which gets a makeover in Yorkshire

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Simon O'Brien recycles some historic oak while Henry Cole comes unstuck when he tries to get to grips with an old Ford Escort

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: The probate detectives try to find the relatives of a south London woman, but they are misled by a trail of dubious clues and are left questioning their knowledge. Meanwhile, research into family trees reveals the lives of British soldiers in 19th-century India

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: Two Brits struggle to turn a leaky barn into a yoga retreat in France, ex-pats welcome their first B&B guests and a couple open a restaurant in a remote Spanish village

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition begins at the Buck Inn in Malham, North Yorkshire, where mother-and-daughter team Karen and Kathryn Souter hope their B&B will impress their guests

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition moves to Smithson Farm in Reedley Hallows, Lancashire, where Carole and Harry Johnson hope their glampsite gives their guests a taste of the great outdoors

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit of the week is to Mallard House in Gilling West, North Yorkshire, where manager Olivia Skeoch and head of housekeeping Teresa Weedon hope their guests will lap up the luxury

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final visit of the week is to Dick and Liz Bradley's B&B, Bryn Derwen in Rhayader, Powys, where it's all about the home-from-home experience. Harry and Carole appear to have found the perfect match but for Kathryn, the decor is disappointing. At dinner, the B&Bers reflect on the week and talk about keeping in touch, but there's an uncomfortable silence from one end of the table

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet for the last time and things get personal when Olivia and Teresa of Mallard House take Harry and Carole to task over their feedback. After dramatic discoveries and cutting comments, the payments are revealed and the winner is announced

programme.title: Grand Designs New Zealand
programme.desc: Cathy and Vince strive to create a massive Italian-style villa with a petanque court and heated pool in Te Arai. However, their budget takes on a life of its own

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: As the festive season approaches at Chester Zoo, Bactrian camels Becky and Mei-Li are about to be given a seasonal surprise in the shape of Sparky, a young addition to the herd. In the zoo's aquarium, Clerk and Doris, long-term mascara barb fish partners, welcome three new females in their tank. Meanwhile, red pandas Nima and Koda are going through a difficult time in their relationship

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Dick and Arthur race to collect low-hanging fruit to make delicious redcurrant jelly. In south-west France, Donna and Paul are hard at work on their most ambitious project yet, creating a tea room. However, stringent building regulations add to the challenge. Elsewhere, Ithaca and Alex need to raise some cash to help repair their chateau's 300-year-old roof

programme.title: Grand Designs: Living in
programme.desc: In the third of four special programmes, Kevin McCloud reveals how self-builders in the suburbs are transforming this most maligned of residential environments. He draws on some of the best examples from the 15-year history of Grand Designs, talks to experts in the latest architectural thinking and reveals the designs that have reinvented the suburbs as a place where home-owners really can have it all

programme.title: Coroner
programme.desc: While the coroner's building is on lockdown, Jenny is isolated from her team with a blast from past. Meanwhile, Detective McAvoy is on the hunt for a newly released killer

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Cameras follow patients at south west London's St George's hospital on royal wedding day. As Harry and Meghan tie the knot, patients and their relatives reflect on the meaning of lifelong love, devotion and commitment. Among those treated are David, 74, who is flown to St George's by air ambulance after falling from a ladder. Medics suspect internal bleeding and he is rushed to CT to assess the damage. His wife Lynda makes her way to the hospital as doctors try to prevent catastrophic blood loss

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the comedy words-and-numbers quiz, with Jon Richardson and James Acaster teaming up against Lee Mack and Sarah Millican. John Cooper Clarke is in Dictionary Corner with Susie Dent, while Rachel Riley looks after the numbers and letters

programme.title: Coroner
programme.desc: While the coroner's building is on lockdown, Jenny is isolated from her team with a blast from past. Meanwhile, Detective McAvoy is on the hunt for a newly released killer

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: In Warwickshire, a 19-year-old man falls from a horse and the 500kg animal then falls on him. The paramedics need to move him with extreme care to the nearest major trauma unit. At the same time in Birmingham, paramedics have to reason with a man determined to void his bowels in the back of the ambulance. Plus, an intoxicated man overdosed on paracetamol, insists on another drink rather than going to hospital

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Cameras follow patients at south west London's St George's hospital on royal wedding day. As Harry and Meghan tie the knot, patients and their relatives reflect on the meaning of lifelong love, devotion and commitment. Among those treated are David, 74, who is flown to St George's by air ambulance after falling from a ladder. Medics suspect internal bleeding and he is rushed to CT to assess the damage. His wife Lynda makes her way to the hospital as doctors try to prevent catastrophic blood loss

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: A compilation of some of the shows previous investigations. Kate heads to the Amazon rainforest to investigate rumours that the Brazil nuts on UK supermarket shelves are all radioactive. Meanwhile, Matt heads out on the streets to find out how the public feel about eating goat meat, and Jimmy takes to the skies above Israel to join the battle to protect the bell pepper crop from the Mediterranean fruit fly

programme.title: Kirstie's Handmade Treasures
programme.desc: Kirstie teams up with cider-maker Andy Thomstone to make a winter version of a popular drink, and demonstrates how to create the Italian favourite limoncello using leftover lemons

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Angela Keenan and Jon Benbow-Brown have visited Menorca five times together, and have fallen in love with the laid-back way of life on the island. The pair are planning to retire there full-time, so Ben Hillman shows them five houses on the island for their budget of £165,000

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole gives Simon O'Brien a motorcycling masterclass in Wiltshire as the duo seek more items to fix up and sell on to make profits for their owners

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien visit the Cotswolds and Somerset, where finds include a set of milk churns and an old munitions shell

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: Teacher Jeffrey Hancock always kept in touch with his relatives, but when the team takes on his case, the pressure is on to track them down. Meanwhile, research into family trees uncovers secret liaisons and the challenges of life as part of the Women's Royal Naval Service, before details of unclaimed estates are disclosed

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: The summer draws to a close for the British ex-pats chasing dream lives abroad. Tempers fray at a new restaurant in a remote Spanish village, and France's newest B&Bers welcome a team of racing car drivers

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition begins at St Hilary Guest House in Llandudno, where owner Tricia Watson and her daughter Jess hope their classic seaside destination will win over their guests

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second of this week's destinations is The Falcon Retreat in south Wales, run by Sue Burston and her right-hand woman Linda Roberts. When the guests arrive at the secluded location, Darren and Alex are wowed by the view, but there are some issues in their room. At dinner, the guests are surprised by their hosts' previous professions, and a conversation about the additional charge for breakfast worries Sue and Linda

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit of the week takes place at The Crown Inn in Grewelthorpe, North Yorkshire, where owners Debbie Heartfield and Paul Dixon hope to impress their guests

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final venue is Stix Glamping in Greetham, Rutland, where one guest decides glamping is not for her before she has even set foot on site, leaving her partner all alone

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet for the last time to find out what they've been paid and to settle some scores. St Hilary Guest House is first and owner Tricia Watson and daughter Jess heat things up over a cold shower. Next, Sue Burston from The Falcon Retreat wants to get her claws into her guests' breakfast blues

programme.title: Grand Designs New Zealand
programme.desc: Sustainability experts Karl and Amelie have a dream to build a home heated from the sun. However, being situated on a steep and windy Wellington hillside site, they are challenged right from the start

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: The fly-on-the-cage-wall documentary returns after a Christmas break. Dominant chimpanzee Dylan is distracted by the females, giving a new generation of males the opportunity to challenge the pecking order. Pudu couple Serena and Odin have a new arrival - but big brother Lucas seems intent on terrorising his new sister - and there is a battle for survival in the aquarium among the Indian mudskipper fish, who can exist both in and out of water

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: The Strawbridges head to the beaches of Saint-Malo with Angel's parents to seek out chateau inspiration. Ithaca and Alex's fundraiser in northern France is a logistical nightmare, but becomes a hot ticket. First impressions are important to Angela and Steve, who get stuck in a last-minute fix as they try to restore their chateau's electric gates before the first rental guests of the season arrive

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud returns to north Cornwall to see one of his favourite Grand Designs, and discover whether Rebecca Sturrock and Gregory Kewish were successful with their ambitious plans. Their project, to reinforce the walls of an old bungalow and put a new living space on top made entirely of cross-laminated timber, soon ran into difficulties, but now fully complete both inside and out, Kevin finds out whether this innovative house is really big enough to accommodate a growing family

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Cameras follow Aydigul, 38, who has suspected sepsis following major abdominal surgery. Her sister is at her bedside throughout, and discusses their childhood in Tajikistan and her extraordinary strength in the face of adversity. Plus, emergency consultant Ahmed rushes to save the life of a woman who has an abdominal aortic aneurysm

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Forty-year-old Tania is rushed to St George's with life-threatening injuries, having been knocked over while crossing a road, while semi-professional footballer Lilli arrives in A&E after being elbowed in the face during a match. Seventy-seven-year-old Edwin is rushed to resus bleeding from a tumour on his neck, and as the medical team examines the wound, his partner Jeanette explains how their romance blossomed in later life. When a maxillofacial specialist arrives to plan the next stage of Edwin's treatment, it forces his family to confront the very painful truth

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: Paramedics Jas Panesar and Amy Cullen rush to a woman who has had a severe allergic reaction at a restaurant. In Birmingham, paramedics Naomi Whatley and Mark McDonagh attend to a man who has fallen unconscious after a severe diabetic attac, while in Warwick, paramedics treat a man who has collapsed with a suspected heart attack

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the words-and-numbers game, with Jon Richardson and Joe Lycett taking on Sean Lock and Michelle Wolf. Adam Riches joins resident lexicographer Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner and Rachel Riley looks after the numbers and letters

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Cameras follow Aydigul, 38, who has suspected sepsis following major abdominal surgery. Her sister is at her bedside throughout, and discusses their childhood in Tajikistan and her extraordinary strength in the face of adversity. Plus, emergency consultant Ahmed rushes to save the life of a woman who has an abdominal aortic aneurysm

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Forty-year-old Tania is rushed to St George's with life-threatening injuries, having been knocked over while crossing a road, while semi-professional footballer Lilli arrives in A&E after being elbowed in the face during a match. Seventy-seven-year-old Edwin is rushed to resus bleeding from a tumour on his neck, and as the medical team examines the wound, his partner Jeanette explains how their romance blossomed in later life. When a maxillofacial specialist arrives to plan the next stage of Edwin's treatment, it forces his family to confront the very painful truth

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Another selection of the team's favourite investigations. Jimmy Doherty visits Thailand to find out if the alarming headlines about additive MSG are justified, Kate Quilton heads to California to find out why there are so many closed pistachios in the packs on supermarket shelves, and Matt Tebbutt learns why slicing onions makes people cry with help from an innovative farmer and a team of cage fighters

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Aussie Pickers
programme.desc: The Pickers get a chance to peruse collector Mingo's stock of vintage Indian Trail and Harley Davidson motorcycles

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: Frank takes a wrong turn when making a delivery and ends up appearing on a quiz show and stumbling on a vintage motorcycle

programme.title: Storage Hunters UK
programme.desc: Dan gambles on a lot he might later regret in when the gang visits Milton Keynes, while Nat lets her heart rule her head on a sale

programme.title: Storage Hunters UK
programme.desc: The fortune-finding show continues in Bentwaters, with the contents of some old army bunkers exciting the bidders, while a vintage box captures Rupert's attention

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: Mike and Frank meet a woman who has a vast collection of valuables but does not like to sell things. They also race to find treasure in a barn before it falls down

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: Mike and Frank meet a man with a collection of classic cars, movie memorabilia and aviator helmets

programme.title: Scrapyard Supercar
programme.desc: Pro driver Shane Lynch allows engineer Ralph Hosier and his teams four days in the scrapyard to build contraptions capable of out-accelerating his iconic Porsche 911 Turbo. Is ingenuity and determination enough to topple one of history's greatest supercars?

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Extended edition. Comedy writer and performer Victoria Wood and Pointless co-host Richard Osman make their debuts on the comedy quiz, joining stand-up Jason Manford and regular panellist Alan Davies. Host Stephen Fry asks a range of questions on the topic of the kitchen sink, with points being awarded for interesting answers as well as correct ones

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Richard Hammond gets behind the wheel of the mid-engined Alfa Romeo 4C on the shores of Lake Como in Italy - but his enjoyment of the lightweight vehicle is disturbed by the arrival of Jeremy Clarkson, who demands a race against his quad bike. Jeremy also visits Belgium, putting the McLaren P1 hypercar to the test in Bruges and around the Spa-Francorchamps Formula One circuit - and its combined power output of more than 900bhp generated from electricity and turbocharged petrol V8 power is likely to cause astonishment. Meanwhile, James May spends time at Camp Bastion in Afghanistan to learn about the army's vehicle redeployment efforts, and Thor star Tom Hiddleston tries to coax the best time he can muster from the Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Rick Stein's Secret France
programme.desc: Rick's tour brings him to Perigord, which is famous for its black truffles and walnuts, as well as duck dishes. He pays a visit to his favourite market in the city of Perigueux, and traces the historic connections between England and the city, which was once known as the Kingdom of Aquitaine. Inspired by his visit, he returns to the kitchen to prepare Enchaud, a traditional pot roast pork from the region, before heading towards the shores of the Mediterranean and the Cote Catalan near the Spanish border

programme.title: Scrapyard Supercar
programme.desc: Pro driver Shane Lynch allows engineer Ralph Hosier and his teams four days in the scrapyard to build contraptions capable of out-accelerating his iconic Porsche 911 Turbo. Is ingenuity and determination enough to topple one of history's greatest supercars?

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: The three petrolheads demonstrate their love of small cars with a trip to Ukraine. Jeremy Clarkson chooses a Volkswagen Up for the journey, while Richard Hammond takes a Ford Fiesta and James May opts for a Dacia Sandero. On their adventure, the trio absorb the history of the Crimean Peninsula, including a visit to an old submarine pen - but the carefree exploration looks likely to become a chore when Jeremy, Richard and James are challenged to a long drive north through the expansive country, during which they must combat serious boredom. And as if that wasn't arduous enough, a new task is set when they arrive in Kiev - a trip into the wasteland of Chernobyl. This edition also features a track test of the Danish Zenvo ST1 supercar and James Blunt returns for another drive in the Reasonably Priced Car

programme.desc: Scott Porter joins Wayne Brady, Greg Proops, Colin Mochrie and Ryan Stiles on the comic improv show

programme.title: Rick Stein's Secret France
programme.desc: Rick's journey takes him to the Spanish border and the Mediterranean coast. He discovers how Catalan and French influences have shaped the culinary legacy of Roussillon in the foothills of the Pyrenees, and samples some of the area's famous mountain cuisine, including grilled sausages, snails and fresh fish caught in a self-contained seawater lake - all with generous servings of the region's famous garlic mayonnaise. Back in his country kitchen, Rick prepares pork and beef meatballs in a tomato and piment sauce

programme.title: Rick Stein's Secret France
programme.desc: The chef concludes his tour with trips to the Languedoc and Provence. programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Richard Hammond visits Abu Dhabi's Yas Marina circuit to drive the Porsche 918 Spyder, a four-wheel-drive hybrid hypercar capable of astonishing speeds thanks to the 886 horsepower it can generate from its screaming V8 petrol engine and electric motors. Jeremy Clarkson and James May join forces to create a public information film to promote safer cycling, with their efforts presented to a panel of experts including members of Westminster Council and Olympic gold medal-winning rider Chris Boardman. Meanwhile, Jeremy - who has promised to change his name to Jennifer if the Porsche is faster around the test track than the McLaren P1 driven earlier in the series - compares the seventh-generation Volkswagen Golf GTI to BMW's critically acclaimed M135i. Breaking Bad star Aaron Paul fires up the Reasonably Priced Car to see what lap time he can achieve

programme.desc: Frank Skinner invites comedian and actor Rob Delaney, presenter Davina McCall and actor and musician Laurence Fox to express their anger over individuals, events and objects that upset them, hoping to see them consigned to oblivion. Their gripes include people who order starters, 12-string guitars and cyclists wearing Lycra

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Extended edition. Sue Perkins, Reginald D Hunter and Jimmy Carr join regular panellist Alan Davies on the comedy quiz. Host Stephen Fry asks a range of fiendish questions on the topic of kitsch, with points being awarded for interesting answers as well as correct ones

programme.title: Jon Richardson: Ultimate Worrier
programme.desc: Jon's wife and fellow comedian Lucy Beaumont is on hand to answer her stressed spouse's worries about himself - and their marriage. Jonathan Ross also guests

programme.title: Late Nite Eats
programme.desc: Jordan Andino heads to Sacramento, where he meets a host of passionate chefs and samples bumpkin - an eggroll stuffed full of brisket and cheese

programme.title: Late Nite Eats
programme.desc: in Oakland, Jordan Andino is blown away by Chef Chris' interpretation of traditional Korean Bibimbap - served with softshell crab, kimchee and grilled pineapple

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Murder Maps
programme.desc: The crimes of Henri Landru, who targeted lonely and vulnerable women during the First World War. He seduced them with promises of marriage and lured them to houses outside Paris where the women vanished. With the police uninterested in investigating the disappearances, two women took it upon themselves to pursue Landru

programme.title: Murder Maps
programme.desc: Herbert Rowse Armstrong, who was convicted of murdering his wife with arsenic. Much of the evidence against him was circumstantial and the scientific testimony remains disputed. This episode examines both sides of the case and asks whether the British justice system made a terrible mistake

programme.title: Forbidden History
programme.desc: Jamie Theakston investigates the Dead Sea Scrolls, visiting the caves where they were discovered to find out who wrote them and why they were hidden

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: The construction and maintenance of the International Space Station, revealing the influence of an unconventional aircraft designer and a 19th-century rebreather

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: The US Navy's fastest combat ships, which are capable of a turn of speed only possible due to a revolutionary water propulsion system and the chance discovery of a brand new alloy

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo reaches the capital on his rail journey from Warwick to Rye in East Sussex. In Ealing, he is transformed for the silver screen by expert hair and make-up, before travelling to Fulham, where he heads underground to explore London's new super sewer. Then, Michael visits the London School of Economics and Political Science in central London, before finally arriving in Kensington

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo journeys from Piccadilly Circus to Gravesend, visiting an exotic West End store that was popular among Victorian women and hearing how a Russian ballet company took London by storm in 1909. He also discovers the origins of netball and gymslips at North Kent College and meets the pilots of the Port of London Authority, founded in the early 20th century

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce visits the Parliament Buildings at Stormont Estate in Belfast, which has played a huge role in Northern Ireland's political history. An unloved muffin dish holds a surprise and Justin Croft spots an unread copy of Ulysses by James Joyce. John Foster delves underground into Belfast's 18th-century water system with an old wooden water pipe, while Adam Schoon meets a man with a phenomenal George Best collection

programme.title: The Railway: First Great Western
programme.desc: The return of the documentary following the work of the train operating company's staff along the route between London, south-west England and south Wales. The first edition focuses on problems caused by last winter's storms, with the worst weather for a century battering the nation and pushing employees and passengers to the limit. Network Rail's Steve Hawkins and his engineers have their work cut out after the line at Dawlish in Devon is washed away, while Dave Slater and his team at Network Control have to battle the elements to keep trains running as storm after storm floods the network

programme.title: Frozen Planet
programme.desc: How animals in the polar regions adapt to the summer weather. In a colony of 400,000 king penguins the adults go surfing and the chicks take mud-baths to cool off, while polar bear cubs take their first swimming lessons as the ice they inhabit melts away. A minke whale is hunted by a family of killer whales, succumbing to their battering after two hours, and Adelie penguins try to prevent their chicks being snatched by skuas

programme.title: Madagascar
programme.desc: David Attenborough observes the wildlife of the desert-like west and south of the island, an area where dry spells can last months and survival is a struggle. Verreaux's sifakas live among trees with potentially lethal spikes, while the baobab trees house mouse lemurs, primates that feed on the droppings of fluffy bugs. During the toughest time of year, gangs of ring-tailed lemurs try to raise their young, and as the rains arrive, the year-long life cycle of the Labord's chameleon begins

programme.title: The Buildings That Fought Hitler
programme.desc: Reigate Fort, which was repurposed to block the Nazis from getting to London and was set to become Resistance HQ should the capital have fallen

programme.title: Nazi Victory: The Post-War Plan
programme.desc: A look at Hitler's plans to build ambitious new cities in the countries he conquered, as well as schemes to transform Berlin to serve as a global capital city

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce visits the Parliament Buildings at Stormont Estate in Belfast, which has played a huge role in Northern Ireland's political history. John Foster delves underground into Belfast's 18th-century water system with an old wooden water pipe, while Adam Schoon meets a man with a phenomenal George Best collection

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo heads from Warwick to Rye in East Sussex. In the orchards of East Malling, Kent, Michael discovers how the Edwardians' serious attitude towards cultivation bears fruit even now. In Folkestone, he hears how the town coped with an influx of more than 100,000 refugees fleeing the German invasion in 1914. In the High Weald, Michael heads for Tenterden Town and the light railway that opened in 1900. His final stop is the medieval Cinque Port of Rye, where he tours the home and garden of author Henry James

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo goes fishing at Ely in Cambridgeshire with the last remaining eel trapper on the Fens, and visits the Denver Sluice, one of the great triumphs of 19th-century engineering. In the Norfolk town of King's Lynn, he uncovers an ambitious plan to reclaim the Wash in Victorian times

programme.title: Underground Worlds
programme.desc: Exploring the military headquarters built beneath Dover Castle during the Second World War and Odessa's catacombs, possibly the largest tunnel network in the world

programme.title: Porridge
programme.desc: Fletcher and the rest of Slade's inmates take advantage of the relaxed atmosphere - and of Barrowclough - with Mackay away on holiday. Ronnie Barker and Richard Beckinsale star

programme.title: Porridge
programme.desc: Peace evades the inmates of Slade Prison, and Fletcher's bid to find a little tranquillity seems destined to fail. Comedy, starring Ronnie Barker and Brian Wilde

programme.title: Porridge
programme.desc: Blanco ends up in the prison hospital, where he fears he is about to shuffle off this mortal coil and entrusts the whereabouts of his stashed fortune to fellow inmate Fletch, who is sharing a ward with him. Comedy, starring Ronnie Barker and David Jason

programme.title: 'Allo 'Allo!
programme.desc: Michelle's cunning plan to release the airmen from the drain in the square is ruined when the gypsy fair is cancelled. Second World War comedy, starring Gorden Kaye and Kirsten Cooke

programme.title: 'Allo 'Allo!
programme.desc: Michelle enlists Rene to set up a mobile radio station to broadcast propaganda, the parts of which will be dropped by plane. Unfortunately, they fall down one of the chateau's chimneys. Second World War comedy, starring Gorden Kaye

programme.title: 'Allo 'Allo!

programme.title: Forbidden History
programme.desc: Jamie Theakston investigates secret societies including the Freemasons, the Bilderberg Group and Bohemia Grove to try and uncover their political influence

programme.title: Impossible Engineering

programme.title: Impossible Engineering

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: A return visit to the historic Abbey Pumping station in Leicester, now the city's Museum of Technology, which complete with four working beam engines is a fine example of Victorian engineering. Expert Ronnie Archer-Morgan swoons over some 1960s steel sculptures, while books specialist Rupert Powell meets playwright Joe Orton's sister who brings in the typewriter on which he wrote some of his most important scripts. Plus, Amin Jaffer discovers a valuable collection of exotic metal boxes - they look like snuff boxes but their actual purpose is much more intriguing. Presented by Fiona Bruce

programme.title: The Railway: First Great Western
programme.desc: As rugby union's Six Nations Championship kicks off in Cardiff, train manager Jodie Edwards and her team try to get thousands of fans home after the match. Penzance in Cornwall remains shut off from the rest of the network as engineers work around the clock to get services running through Dawlish, where the line was washed away, while new duty manager Joe has his hands full on his first Saturday night shift at London's Paddington station when the last Wales-bound train of the evening has a problem

programme.title: Frozen Planet
programme.desc: With the arrival of autumn the Arctic Ocean begins freezing over, and polar bears gather in large numbers on the coast as they await the return of the ice. Two thousand beluga whales head for a particular estuary to thrash against the gravel and exfoliate, while in Antarctica, Adelie penguins take to the sea for the first time and face the danger of leopard seal attacks

programme.title: The Life of Mammals
programme.desc: David Attenborough presents an insight into the most successful animals on the planet. Observing an Arctic fox stalk prey in its -20C environment, he reveals warm-bloodedness as one of the key factors in the mammals' global domination. He also studies marsupials in Australia and Central America, offering a unique view inside a platypus's breeding burrow

programme.title: The Buildings That Fought Hitler
programme.desc: With the Blitz taking its toll, Britain had to keep its manufacturing going. Shadow factories were born and were crucial in the production of ammunition

programme.title: Nazi Victory: The Post-War Plan

programme.title: Secret Nazi Bases

programme.title: The Railway: First Great Western
programme.desc: Cheltenham Spa station manager Richard Morrish braces himself for a hectic four days with the two-platform station used by more than 60,000 horse racing fans during the Cheltenham Festival. Just as the mainline at Dawlish is about to be reopened after being washed away in the winter storms, a crack is discovered in the sandstone cliffs two miles down the line and a landslide threatens to engulf the tracks. Conductor Jameil and driver Gary have to react quickly on the Reading to Gatwick service when a pedestrian takes a perilous short cut

programme.title: Frozen Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough examines how the arrival of winter sparks a ruthless battle for survival in the polar regions, with temperatures plummeting to -70C, winds reaching 120mph and day-long darkness. As a blizzard rages, a polar bear treks into the Arctic mountains to give birth, while the entire world population of spectacled eider braves the weather in a giant ice hole. Wolves and bison engage in battle, but alliances are also made, and beneath the snow, voles try to dodge the attacks of great grey owls and weasels

programme.title: The Life of Mammals
programme.desc: David Attenborough offers an insight into insect-eating animals, including shrews that dive under water, moles that can 'swim' in the Namib Desert, and New Zealand bats that hunt in packs on the ground, walking on their wrists and foraging. Plus, how hedgehogs and armadillos liven up their diet with fruit and birds' eggs, while pangolins and giant anteaters survive entirely on a diet of insects

programme.title: The Buildings That Fought Hitler

programme.desc: Pia and Stephen Abbott have been married for 35 years and have long dreamt of owning a property in Spain. Jasmine Harman helps the couple find a home around La Marina Alta within their £130,000 budget

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Fitness fanatic Jen Edwards throws the first in a series of parties in Swansea, but despite claiming to be a confident cook, she fumbles with her choice of starter. programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Teacher James Knight hosts a school-themed party in Swansea, complete with children's games and bangers and mash. programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Rugby player Keith Larrard hosts as the competition in Swansea reaches the midway point. He hopes to impress his guests with a Jackson 5 theme and a naked butler, but his guests are confused by a cryptic menu with dishes including Shoulder Sheep Sh'Moan

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Karen Wood hosts the fourth dinner party challenge in Swansea and hopes to impress not only with her buffet-style menu, but also by providing a hot tub. programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Solicitor Katherine Waldemar Brown hosts the final party of the competition in Swansea, and asks her guests to adhere to a tarts-and-vicars dress code. programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay tries to help a struggling seafood restaurant in Manhattan, where the three owners regularly come into conflict as they each try to assert their authority over the business, causing confusion for all. When one of the trio stubbornly resists the changes the Scottish chef is hoping to implement, sparks begin to fly

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Dick Strawbridge and Angel Adoree give a magnificent five-metre-high staircase a makeover with help from design experts Dorothy and Arthur. programme.title: A Place in the Sun

programme.title: Britain's Most Luxurious Hotels

In Sheffield city centre, officers help a confused elderly woman suffering from dementia who cannot find her way home

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Fiery chef Gordon Ramsay comes to the aid of the owners of Hannah & Mason's, a French bistro in Cranbury, New Jersey. programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA

programme.desc: The chef takes on one of his toughest challenges yet, J Willy's Bar and Grille in South Bend, Indiana, which has amassed debts of more than $1million. On finding an inexperienced chef serving food from both the freezer and tins, together with service that reduces one customer to tears, Gordon Ramsay orders an overnight deep-clean of the premises and designs a menu to inspire the kitchen staff

Seven-year-old Dobermann Dexter has serious spinal problems, collapsing on his back legs and tumbling on his front legs. His owners meet Noel to discuss options for his treatment

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

Following a series of tantalising clues, Emilia and David narrow the field of suspects down to just one man. Will he reveal the truth?

programme.title: Britain's Most Luxurious Hotels

Crime thriller, starring Lesley Sharp and Patrick Gibson

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet up for a final showdown. programme.title: A Place in the Sun

programme.desc: Sharon Taylor and John Healey both have busy teaching jobs in London, and long to spend a bit more quality time together. programme.desc: Feminist and student Candida Darling continues the challenge on the Wirral. The views she holds on Vernal Aley's lap-dancing business are questioned when the guests discover a set of revealing pictures, but the night ends in good spirits

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Chicken enthusiast Adele Richards intends to use an abundance of home-grown ingredients, including freshly laid eggs, when she hosts the third party on the Wirral. programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Hairdresser Chrissy Griffin hosts the fourth dinner party on the Wirral, Merseyside, hoping to win over her guests with a Caribbean menu and a group of Spanish troubadours. However, tensions soon boil over and one of the party storms out

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Project manager Stuart Leake hosts the final dinner party on the Wirral and hopes to bring harmony to the group with a 1920s-themed event. He also gives a demonstration of his piano playing, before the contestants discover who will be taking home the prize money

programme.desc: Tracey and Dean met in their early twenties and have devoted their lives to raising their family, including their own three children and two foster children. programme.title: George Clarke's Amazing Spaces
programme.desc: Architect George Clarke delves into the world of small builds, meeting people taking tiny, unpromising spaces and creating places to live, work and play, demonstrating how big dreams can be achieved in small and affordable places. He also looks for inspiration on how to put a garden inside his rotating home

Claire and Matt are looking for a quirky old home with open-plan living and are not afraid of a project, while Sadie and Simon have sold their home so they need a new place pronto - but bungalows in Bath are not easy to find. Rich and Bethan have missed out on two properties in 12 months, and their desire for chocolate box perfection is stronger than ever

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud meets Greg and Georgie who are taking on the highly ambitious task of converting a dilapidated, 35-year-old barn in Kent. The build will be a huge challenge involving the painstaking conservation of the existing rotten structure, building a new two-storey internal steel frame and recycling everything they can to stretch their £250,000 budget

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: A 23-year-old virgin from Kent picks a partner from six nude candidates - but this proves a nerve-racking experience, since he has never seen a naked woman in his life. Meanwhile, a 27-year-old beauty therapist from London is determined to have a baby before she turns 30, and hopes that one of the men lurking behind the screens will be the perfect father

programme.title: Celebrity Gogglebox
programme.desc: A rolling cast of famous faces, some of Britain's best loved personalities, turn their hand to being the country's most opinionated viewers, sharing their opinions on what they have been watching during the week

programme.title: Celebrity Gogglebox
programme.desc: Famous faces, including Denise Van Outen, Nick Grimshaw, Mo Gilligan, Rylan Clark-Neal, Gyles Brandreth, Shaun Ryder, and Martin and Roman Kemp, turn their hand to being the country's most opinionated viewers, sharing their thoughts on what they have been watching during the week

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A 10-year-old Dobermann is treated for an aggressive bone tumour in its leg. One option is a full limb amputation, but another could potentially save Rolo's leg by cutting out the affected part of the bone and replacing it with a unique implant of Noel's own invention - a scapula-humeral endoprosthesis. Five-year-old labrador Jeff is suffering from developmental elbow disease, which cannot be managed by medication alone. The day of his planned surgery for a full elbow replacement arrives, though bad news places the prospect of such treatment in jeopardy

programme.title: Bake Off: The Professionals
programme.desc: Six new teams enter the kitchen and for their first challenge they must produce two different types of miniature classics, the lemon meringue pie and the tiramisu - 24 perfect examples of each in three and a half hours. After that judges Benoit Blin and Cherish Finden ask them to take the humble fruit crumble and transform it into a fine-dining showpiece, complete with towering, edible sculptures. Presented by Tom Allen and Liam Charles

programme.title: We Are Lady Parts
programme.desc: The band are buzzing from the gig and excited about how influencer Zarina's magazine article could open things up for them. Meanwhile, Amina is feeling more confident and empowered than ever, comfortably living both versions of herself. However, when the magazine article drops early, it's not what the band expected and, after it goes viral online, there's a whole world of negativity towards them. Lady Parts are now famous, but the cost may be too hard to bear. Comedy, with Anjana Vasan, Juliette Motamed, Lucie Shorthouse and Sarah Kameela Impey

Female impersonator James and his partner David have rented a working farm, and it proves a steep learning curve for the pair who have sunk every penny into the project

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Two officers make a surprising discovery while apprehending a gang of car thieves, and a team uses automatic number-plate recognition to identify stolen vehicles outside the biggest shopping centre in Essex. An air ambulance makes an emergency landing in Clacton-on-Sea, a three-car shunt brings the M25 to a halt and a policeman drops his handcuffs down a drain

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Officers conduct a 48-hour hunt for a thief who stole a 4x4, and their perseverance pays off when they manage to catch the culprit. Meanwhile, in Clacton-on-Sea, a tip-off sends the interceptors to a drug den, and the Essex chopper team co-ordinates a manhunt when three high-profile suspects evade capture by fleeing into a field full of cows

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: The team is forced to don full riot gear on New Year's Eve to deal with a knife-wielding man, and unprecedented snowfall brings chaos to the roads of Essex. Meanwhile, an officer seizes a haul of mystery drugs, and the Air Support Unit lends a hand in a chase across six lanes of traffic

programme.title: Dogs Behaving (Very) Badly

programme.title: Home and Away

programme.desc: At Holiday Resort Unity in Somerset, there's a surprise in store for soft play worker Lauren as Covid-19 restrictions are lifted and venues are reopened - and entertainment chief Chunkie Russell is finally allowed to welcome the guests into the main entertainment venue RJs. Meanwhile, at Tencreek Holiday Park in Looe, a flash flood causes chaos in the kitchen - and the Knowles family from Manchester embark on their annual crabbing competition

programme.title: Dogs Behaving (Very) Badly
programme.desc: Graeme Hall comes to the aid of a German Shepherd working as a security dog at a nightclub, who gets confused about who exactly he is supposed to protect the business against. Other clients include a labradoodle who objects to her owners doing any housework and a dispute between the dogs belonging to two generations of the same family

programme.title: Filthy House SOS
programme.desc: Richard and Brennan help a 75-year-old widow from Dudley to sort out her filthy home, who had little time for housework when caring for her husband through a terminal illness

programme.title: The Gypsies Next Door
programme.desc: Documentary about gypsies fighting to keep their traditions alive and the locals trying to ban them. Traveller Tony dreams of a new life for his family so he has set up a sunbed business with millionaire non-traveller Colin. However, the neighbours have got a court order to prevent him setting up the company. Margaret, a single mother of seven, just needs a roof over her head after being evicted, while Simey Connors prepares a grand funeral for his brother Mikey, a legend in the traveller community

programme.title: Adults Only
programme.desc: People making a living from selling sex, including a couple who run their burgeoning porn film business from their house in Southend while also looking after their children, and a 70 -year-old dominatrix working from a dungeon in her semi-detached house in Swindon

programme.title: The Sex Business
programme.desc: A disparate group of young sex workers reveal what life is really like trying to make a living as escorts, cammers and sex entrepreneurs. The film explores why these young women decided to 'stop giving it away for free', revealing in the process how materialism has collided with a new age of sexual pragmatism

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: The programme follows officers from Derbyshire's road crime unit as they target dealers bringing drugs into the county, with PC Nick Lovatt giving chase after a sighting of a suspected dealer in Derby. At police HQ, the cops receive intelligence about a van travelling towards Derbyshire suspected of carrying a large amount of cash. The Road Crime Unit heads into position to intercept the van and surround it on the motorway

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: Derbyshire's Road Crime Unit uses intelligence and surveillance tactics to crack down on suspected drug dealers. The team receives some intelligence about a man suspected of ferrying drugs into the county using a relative's car. After boxing the car to a stop, a struggle breaks out between the driver and road crime cop Nick Lovatt, and the suspect is forcibly cuffed and detained

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Emily and Grant join forces with Ofcom to raid a pirate radio station, and Biggers and Andy track down a suspected drug runner on the M25. At a national car event, the Mitsubishi Evo 10 police vehicle proves to be popular with thousands of auto enthusiasts

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Officers track down the people responsible for stealing an expensive TV, and carry out an impairment test on a driver suspected of being under the influence of drugs. Elsewhere, three men armed with a suspicious toolkit are stopped for questioning, and interceptors test their skills on a driving simulator

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Officers are kept busy with public order patrols as World Cup fever hits Essex, and an ambitious thief tries to steal a 200-gallon fuel tank. programme.title: Bargain-Loving Brits in the Sun
programme.desc: Documentary following British people living it up on the Costa del Sol. Bambi shows newcomers Howard and Lynne around the caravan park and treats them to a Bollywood evening. But unaware to the couple the host has recently gone vegan, so it turns out to be a meat-free knees-up. Kim and Matt reopen their chip shop after a devastating fire which forced them to shut for a year and Harrison is down on the marina, trying to sell tickets for the Black Pearl pirate ship tour

programme.title: Casualty 24/7: Every Second Counts
programme.desc: Documentary following the work of medical emergency teams in the A&E department of Barnsley Hospital. Paramedics rush in Dean, a 30-year-old who has been admitted after being in a serious motorbike collision and may have spinal injuries. Elsewhere, doctors treat a 20-year-old with severe abdominal pain, and a 90-year-old with an injured finger

programme.title: 999: Critical Condition
programme.desc: Return of the documentary following the work of medical staff at Stoke's Royal University Hospital, as they strive to save the lives of sick and injured patients. A one-punch attack in the street leaves a man with a potentially devastating head injury, and trauma team leader Richard quickly realises he needs to perform emergency surgery on a bleed inside his brain. A young man arrives with multiple broken bones and suspected internal injuries following a motorbike accident, while a factory worker hopes the team can save his arm after a horrific accident with a circular saw

programme.title: A&E After Dark
programme.desc: After being run over by a car on her birthday, 73-year-old Sue arrives by ambulance with spinal and head injuries, and Dr Bijou and his team have to act quickly to save her life. programme.title: Police Interceptors

programme.desc: Gibbs is made acting director while Jenny is attending an overseas conference. Drama, starring Mark Harmon and Sean Murray

programme.title: Law & Order

programme.desc: Bernard and Lupo investigate when a stockbroker admitted to hospital dies on the operating table from severe internal bleeding. When the case reaches court, McCoy is forced to make one of the most controversial arguments of his career and his path crosses with ADA Cutter

programme.title: NCIS

They discover a bullet wound in the body, as well as another victim at the bottom of a nearby ravine - a woman who was beaten to death. Ducky conducts an autopsy and makes a discovery that sets the agents off in search of a missing infant. Meanwhile, Tony is distracted by developments in his relationship with Jeanne

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: After a teenager is found dead at a high-school party, the detectives go to great lengths to secure convictions against the two leading suspects. To complicate matters, a juror's attraction to Connie looks set to disrupt the subsequent trial. Drama, starring Alana De La Garza

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A psychotherapist is shot dead, and his wife stands trial for murder when it is revealed the man was involved in an inappropriate relationship with one of his teenage clients. However, when the widow reveals she was also one of the doctor's victims in her youth, the DA's office is forced to prove that childhood traumas are no excuse for killing someone

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Brimstone and Curtis investigate an attack on a nurse, and discover she had been moving house regularly in an effort to evade a stalker who terrorised her with distressing e-mails. The victim regains consciousness, but fails to identify her attacker, leading to the shocking revelation that she may have fabricated the assault story as a way of attracting attention

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A man is found murdered in a local park and the death is initially linked to drugs, but as Bernard and Lupo investigate they discover fingerprints at the scene that lead them to a man with learning difficulties who holds the key to the killing

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Lupo and Bernard try to solve the murder of a 17-year-old youth, and discover his younger brother had recently been banished from a polygamous Mormon sect. As they investigate the group's controversial leader, the detectives learn he had been trying to track down one of his wives. Crime drama, starring Jeremy Sisto, Anthony Anderson and Sam Waterston, with Colm Meaney (Star Trek: The Next Generation)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Paramedics become suspicious about a baby's death, calling in Brimstone and Curtis, who discover the parents adopted the child from an illegal Russian ring. The case takes on another dimension altogether when it transpires that the crooks specialise in placing terminally ill infants with unsuspecting families. Starring Jerry Orbach and Benjamin Bratt

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: While performing an autopsy for a class of military students, Ducky discovers the cadaver he is using is the body of a murder victim. In trying to identify the corpse, Gibbs and his team are set on the trail of a career criminal specialising in identity fraud. Meanwhile, Ziva has difficulty separating her personal feelings and professional judgment. Starring David McCallum and Mark Harmon

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A Navy Seal is shot dead while threatening to throw himself from the top of a building. An al-Qaeda operative emerges as the main suspect, but DiNozzo thinks the killer may actually be found closer to home. Elsewhere, a rumour spreads that Abby may be about to leave NCIS for good. Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette star in the American drama

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The unit is called to examine the mysterious death of a sailor serving on a research vessel engaged in a top-secret mission. The ship proves to have been abandoned by its crew, and Ducky discovers the victim died from a highly contagious virus - putting everyone presently on board at risk. Drama, starring David McCallum, Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Lauren Holly

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: A woman accusing a popular athlete of rape takes to a billboard to release her frustration when the investigation is stalled

programme.title: Law & Order: Criminal Intent
programme.desc: Goren and Eames investigate the murder of a diver of Middle Eastern descent whose body has been found washed ashore on Coney Island. Initial inquiries indicate the dead man may have been planning a terrorist attack, but the detectives later find themselves caught up in a plot to prevent the truth being revealed

programme.title: Law & Order: Criminal Intent
programme.desc: A corrupt judge's wife is shot dead and Logan and Falacci's investigations soon lead them to a string of suspects, all with tangled motives. Among those in the frame are a former CIA operative, an FBI agent and an actress-director couple in the middle of a bitter divorce. Drama, guest starring Steve Guttenberg, with Chris Noth

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: The Bakers, the stars of America's top reality TV show, are rocked by scandal when 13-year-old daughter Lane is discovered to be three months pregnant. Ultra-conservative parents Pam and Frank resist SVU involvement in a misguided attempt to shield their 10 children - and their lucrative TV empire - from disgrace. However, when Lane accuses one of the show's cameramen of raping her, one who happens to have already had a vasectomy, it becomes clear the Bakers are trying to protect someone. Unable to aid in the inquiry due to her own pregnancy, Rollins decides to make good use of her time away from the beat to binge-watch the Bakers' show in an effort to identify potential suspects. Crime drama, starring Mariska Hartigay

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: The hunt for a missing teenage girl takes an emotional toll on Sgt Dodds, and DNA evidence becomes the crucial link the team needs to identify the youngster's abductor. Hard-hitting and emotional series chronicling the life and crimes of the New York Police Department, starring Mariska Hargitay

programme.title: Bull
programme.desc: The team members fear that they're in over their heads when they take on the seemingly unwinnable case of Elliott Miles, a mute man facing the death penalty if he's found guilty of murder and arson. Elsewhere, Bull is stunned to learn that his ex-wife is remarrying

programme.title: Bull
programme.desc: Jason's team is rocked when client Elliott Miles is found guilty of murder, and they must regroup as the trial enters the penalty phase in order to prevent him from being sentenced to death. But when new evidence emerges that could prove Elliott's innocence, Bull must find a way to present it in court without being found in contempt. Meanwhile, Marissa faces hard truths about her relationship with Bull

programme.title: Criminals: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: Journalist Nick Wallis joins police forces around the nation to discover how CCTV is helping to bring criminals using weapons to justice. A CCTV operator's instincts lead him to keep an eye on a man acting suspiciously on a train station platform and who then takes a knife out of his pocket and threatens a woman coming towards him. There is also footage of a raid on a west London jewellery shop, with the gang spraying bleach in the eyes of security guards and smashing down the doors with pickaxes while the terrified staff cower inside

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Police action to close down an illegal dog-fighting ring leads to a murder inquiry when officers find a woman's finger inside one of the creatures. As the case deepens, Detectives Green and Lupo must contend with the involvement of a pushy journalist. Crime drama, guest starring Lara Flynn Boyle (The Practice)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Lupo and Green investigate when an adulterous art dealer is stoned to death. The detectives learn that the victim's plan to display provocative art led to unwanted attention from a terrorist organisation and gives them cause to suspect her son - a fanatical Christian - was persuaded by a religious leader to kill his mother. Police drama, starring Sam Waterston

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Brimstone and Curtis investigate the disappearance of a wealthy couple seemingly snatched from their apartment building, but suspicion eventually falls on a man suffering from mental illness - presenting Ross with a tough ethical decision. Police drama, starring Jerry Orbach, Benjamin Bratt and Carey Lowell

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Lupo and Bernard investigate when a man is crushed to death under a crane. Initially they suspect the construction company of foul play, but the case leads them to the victim's hospitalised wife, as well as plans for a controversial medical procedure

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Lupo and Bernard investigate the murder of a man visiting New York City to confront his past, but as they delve into the victim's personal life, the case becomes complicated when all clues point to a revered sheriff. Meanwhile, McCoy faces competition when a new district attorney is nominated for election

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: McCoy and Carmichael have the unenviable task of prosecuting three police officers with a racially motivated hate crime that threatens to cause uproar in the city. Police drama, starring Sam Waterston and Angie Harmon

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A childhood friend of Gibbs' dead daughter is stalked by a marine who recently returned from Iraq, and asks the agent for help. Memories of his murdered family lead him to make the case a priority, but he acts on his initiative and leaves HQ without his badge, making DiNozzo fear he is risking his job for a personal vendetta. Mark Harmon and Michael Weatherly star

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A taxi driver and an admiral working at the Pentagon are found executed by the side of a road, but the agents have trouble ascertaining a motive - until they uncover links to a woman searching for her missing husband, a political refugee from Africa. Meanwhile, McGee makes a discovery about his new girlfriend. Starring Sean Murray, Michael Weatherly and Mark Harmon

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Abby babysits a bright nine-year-old while the other members of the team try to locate the youngster's missing father - a survivalist wanted in connection with a case of robbery and murder. The search takes them deep into the wilderness - where a startling discovery awaits. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Sean Murray

programme.title: Columbo: Negative Reaction (1974)
programme.desc: A successful photographer wants to get rid of his nagging wife so he can pursue an affair with his assistant. He murders his spouse and makes it look as if she was killed by a kidnapper, and at the same time puts an ex-con in the frame. However, the dogged detective sees through his ruse and proceeds to pick the story to pieces. Crime drama, with Peter Falk, Dick Van Dyke, Don Gordon and Antoinette Bower

programme.title: Columbo: Now You See Him (1976)
programme.desc: A stage magician has kept the fact that he is a Nazi war criminal a secret for years. When a nightclub manager threatens to expose him, the illusionist kills him and arranges the perfect alibi - because at the time of the murder, he was apparently performing an escapology act. However, Columbo sets out to uncover the truth. Detective drama, with Peter Falk and Jack Cassidy

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: Benson hopes justice will be served as child abductor Lewis Hodda goes on trial. However, when a plea deal is rejected, Barba must rely on decades-old evidence to make his case, and faces off against former SVU colleague Dr Huang. Crime drama, guest starring Tom Sizemore

programme.title: Gone
programme.desc: Fifteen years after her abduction as a child, Kick Lannigan teams up with the FBI agent who rescued her to search for a missing girl. Pilot episode of the crime drama, starring Chris Noth and Leven Rambin

programme.title: Star Trek: The Next Generation
programme.desc: Troi's mother falls in love with a scientist from a planet where inhabitants are expected to die at 60, and persuades him to defy his people by living - unwittingly pushing the Enterprise to the brink of war. Guest starring Majel Barrett

programme.title: Star Trek: Enterprise
programme.desc: T'Pol joins the crew on a visit to an ancient Vulcan monastery, despite objecting that her human shipmate's presence could be a disruptive one. On landing, they are shocked to discover the spiritual sanctuary has been taken over by the Andorians, a paranoid race of aliens with a long history of conflict with the Vulcan people. Sci-fi adventure, starring Scott Bakula and Jolene Blalock

programme.title: Star Trek: Deep Space Nine

programme.title: Stargate Atlantis

Sci-fi drama, starring Joe Flanigan and Rachel Luttrell

programme.title: Star Trek: The Next Generation
programme.desc: Dr Crusher falls hopelessly in love with an alien peace ambassador, only for him to be critically injured in an attack. However, when the lovestruck medic examines the object of her affections, she discovers a shocking secret - his handsome exterior is merely a host for the small purple parasite within - and what's more, he needs a new body to survive. Sci-fi adventure, starring Gates McFadden

programme.title: Star Trek: Enterprise

programme.title: Star Trek: The Next Generation
programme.desc: Geordi is kidnapped by Romulans on his way to a conference and he is subjected to days of brainwashing designed to turn him into a remorseless killing machine. Returning to the Enterprise a few days later, with no recollection of his ordeal, the engineer remains unaware of his part in a plot to assassinate a high-ranking governor who suspects the Federation of aiding a group of Klingon rebels

programme.title: Star Trek: Enterprise

programme.title: A Year at Kew
programme.desc: Ray Townsend sets off on a field trip to an island 30 miles off Japan, hoping to break Tony Kirkham's feat of bringing back 155 different sets of seeds on a single expedition. Meanwhile, staff are finding new uses for discarded pairs of tights, and Phil Griffiths discovers a huge fallen tree limb suitable for one of his displays, but the half-ton branch must be manoeuvred into position in the Princess of Wales Conservatory

programme.title: Doctor in the House (1954)

Comedy based on the novel by Richard Gordon, starring Dirk Bogarde, Kenneth More, James Robertson Justice and Donald Sinden

programme.title: The Maggie (1954)
programme.desc: A brash American businessman hires a small Scottish company to move a set of valuable furniture to his new home on a remote island. However, he soon learns that his belongings are to be transported on a dilapidated boat in desperate need of repair - and sets out in hot pursuit of them. Ealing comedy, with Paul Douglas, Alex Mackenzie, James Copeland and Tommy Kearins
